symptoms: dynamic routes not working in production
root_cause: Dynamic routes not configured correctly or missing generateStaticParams for static export
fix: Ensure dynamic route files use correct bracket notation: [slug]/page.tsx
Add generateStaticParams() for statically generated dynamic routes
Check next.config.js for incorrect rewrites or redirects

symptoms: API works locally but fails on Vercel
root_cause: Edge runtime incompatibility or missing environment variables in Vercel
fix: Check Vercel dashboard for environment variables - ensure they are set for the correct environment (Production/Preview/Development)
Verify API route is not using Node.js-only APIs with edge runtime
Remove 'export const runtime = "edge"' if using Node.js specific modules

symptoms: text content does not match server-rendered HTML
root_cause: Component renders different content on server vs client, often due to browser-only APIs or date/time formatting
fix: Wrap browser-only code in useEffect or use dynamic import with { ssr: false }
Use suppressHydrationWarning for intentional mismatches like timestamps
Ensure consistent date formatting using UTC or a library like date-fns
Check for extensions or injected scripts that modify DOM

root_cause: Image domain not configured in next.config.js or loader misconfiguration
fix: Add remote image domains to next.config.js under images.remotePatterns
Example: images: { remotePatterns: [{ protocol: 'https', hostname: 'example.com' }] }
For external CDN, configure a custom loader or use unoptimized prop

root_cause: Middleware matcher misconfigured or infinite redirect loop due to missing path exclusions
fix: Check middleware.ts matcher config to ensure correct path patterns
Exclude API routes and static files: matcher: ['/((?!api|_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)']
Add condition to skip redirect if already on target path

root_cause: Serverless function exceeds Vercel's default 10s timeout on Hobby plan (60s on Pro)
fix: Optimize database queries and add connection pooling
Increase maxDuration in route config: export const maxDuration = 30
Move long-running tasks to background jobs using Vercel Cron or a queue
Upgrade to Pro plan for up to 300s timeout

root_cause: Edge function using unsupported Node.js APIs or exceeding memory/size limits
fix: Remove Node.js-specific imports (fs, path, crypto with certain methods)
Switch to Node.js runtime: export const runtime = 'nodejs'
Reduce bundle size - edge functions have a 1MB limit after compression
Use edge-compatible alternatives: crypto.subtle instead of crypto

root_cause: Server-only module imported in client component or edge runtime
fix: Add 'use server' directive or move import to server component
Use next/dynamic with ssr: false for client-only imports
Add 'server-only' package to prevent accidental client imports: import 'server-only'
Check that server utilities aren't re-exported from barrel files used by client components

root_cause: Serverless function cannot reach external service or is using localhost URL in production
fix: Replace localhost URLs with production URLs using environment variables
Use NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix only for client-side env vars; use server-side env vars in API routes
Ensure external service allows connections from Vercel IP ranges
Add proper error handling and retry logic for transient network failures

root_cause: NextAuth.js not configured with correct NEXTAUTH_URL for Vercel deployment
fix: Set NEXTAUTH_URL to your production domain in Vercel environment variables
Set NEXTAUTH_SECRET using: openssl rand -base64 32
For Vercel, NEXTAUTH_URL can be omitted if using VERCEL_URL (auto-set by Vercel)
Ensure callback URLs in OAuth provider match the production domain

symptoms: Warning: Each child in a list should have a unique key prop
root_cause: React list items missing unique key prop or using array index as key with dynamic lists
fix: Add unique key prop to each element in map(): items.map(item => <div key={item.id}>)
Use stable unique identifiers, not array indices, for dynamic lists
If no unique ID exists, generate one at data creation time, not render time

symptoms: Warning: Cannot update a component while rendering a different component
root_cause: State update triggered during render phase instead of in effect or event handler
fix: Move state updates into useEffect() or event handlers
Use useCallback for memoized event handlers that trigger state updates
If derived state is needed, compute it during render without setState

symptoms: works locally but not in CI
root_cause: Node.js version mismatch or missing build configuration
fix: Set Node.js version in package.json engines field or Vercel project settings
Add .nvmrc file with target version (e.g., 20.x)
Check that all dependencies are in dependencies, not just devDependencies if needed at runtime
Set installCommand or buildCommand in vercel.json if using monorepo

root_cause: API routes missing CORS headers for cross-origin requests
fix: Add CORS headers in API route or middleware
In route handler: return new Response(body, { headers: { 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin': '*', 'Access-Control-Allow-Methods': 'GET,POST,OPTIONS' } })
Handle OPTIONS preflight: if (req.method === 'OPTIONS') return new Response(null, { status: 204, headers: corsHeaders })
Use vercel.json headers config for static assets

root_cause: Client-side env vars missing NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix or not included at build time
fix: Prefix client-side variables with NEXT_PUBLIC_: NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL
Server-side only vars (API keys, secrets) should NOT have NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix
Redeploy after adding env vars - they are baked in at build time for client
Use Vercel CLI: vercel env pull .env.local to sync environment variables

root_cause: Incorrect revalidation configuration or cache headers misconfigured
fix: Set revalidate in fetch options: fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 60 } })
Use revalidatePath() or revalidateTag() in Server Actions for on-demand revalidation
For App Router: export const revalidate = 60 in page/layout
Check Vercel dashboard Data Cache section for cache hit/miss metrics

root_cause: Serverless environments create new connections per invocation without pooling
fix: Use connection pooling: PgBouncer for PostgreSQL, Prisma Data Proxy, or Vercel Postgres
For Prisma: use @prisma/client with connection pooling URL (port 6543 for Supabase)
Cache PrismaClient instance: globalThis.prisma = globalThis.prisma || new PrismaClient()
Consider Vercel KV (Redis) or Vercel Postgres for Vercel-native options

root_cause: Vercel serverless functions have a 4.5MB body size limit on request payload
fix: Use presigned URLs to upload directly to S3/Cloudflare R2 from the client
Configure API route body parser: export const config = { api: { bodyParser: { sizeLimit: '4mb' } } }
For App Router: export const maxDuration and use streaming
Use Vercel Blob Storage for simple file upload needs

root_cause: Need to implement route protection with proper session management
fix: Use NextAuth.js (Auth.js) or Clerk for managed authentication
Protect server components: const session = await getServerSession(authOptions)
Protect API routes: check session at start of handler
Use middleware.ts for route-level protection before page loads
Store session tokens in httpOnly cookies, not localStorage

symptoms: how to fetch data in server component
root_cause: Need to implement efficient data fetching with proper caching and error handling
fix: Fetch in Server Components directly: async function Page() { const data = await fetch(url) }
Use React cache() for request deduplication across components
Implement loading.tsx and error.tsx for Suspense boundaries
Use parallel data fetching with Promise.all() for independent requests
Set appropriate revalidation: fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 3600, tags: ['posts'] } })

symptoms: form submission in Next.js App Router
root_cause: Need to implement mutations/form handling with Server Actions
fix: Define server action with 'use server' directive at top of function or file
Use in form: <form action={serverAction}>
For programmatic use: call server action from client with useTransition
Revalidate data after mutation: revalidatePath('/path') or revalidateTag('tag')
Return typed responses: use zod for validation, return { error } or { success } objects

root_cause: Need comprehensive error handling for both server and client errors
fix: Create app/error.tsx (client component) for route segment error boundaries
Create app/global-error.tsx for root layout errors
Create app/not-found.tsx for 404 pages
Use try/catch in Server Actions and return error state to client
Integrate error reporting: Sentry, LogRocket, or Vercel Web Analytics

symptoms: implement rate limiting on API routes
root_cause: API routes need rate limiting to prevent abuse in production
fix: Use Vercel KV (Redis) for distributed rate limiting
Install @upstash/ratelimit: npm install @upstash/ratelimit @upstash/redis
Code: const ratelimit = new Ratelimit({ redis: Redis.fromEnv(), limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(10, '10s') })
In handler: const { success } = await ratelimit.limit(identifier); if (!success) return Response.json({ error: 'Too many requests' }, { status: 429 })
Use IP from headers: request.headers.get('x-forwarded-for') ?? '127.0.0.1'

root_cause: Need to run periodic tasks in serverless environment
fix: Create API route: app/api/cron/route.ts
Add to vercel.json: { "crons": [{ "path": "/api/cron", "schedule": "0 */6 * * *" }] }
Verify request is from Vercel Cron: if (req.headers.get('Authorization') !== `Bearer ${process.env.CRON_SECRET}`) return Response.json({}, { status: 401 })
Set CRON_SECRET env var in Vercel dashboard
Pro plan required for cron intervals less than once per day

root_cause: Need to revalidate cached pages when CMS content changes
fix: Create revalidation API route: app/api/revalidate/route.ts
Use revalidateTag: import { revalidateTag } from 'next/cache'; revalidateTag('blog-posts');
Or revalidatePath: import { revalidatePath } from 'next/cache'; revalidatePath('/blog');
Secure endpoint with secret: if (req.headers.get('x-webhook-secret') !== process.env.REVALIDATION_SECRET) return Response.json({ message: 'Invalid secret' }, { status: 401 })
Configure CMS webhook to hit /api/revalidate on content publish

root_cause: Multiple potential causes for performance degradation
fix: Check Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights for real user metrics
Run Lighthouse audit and check LCP, FID/INP, CLS scores
Identify blocking resources: check if large JS bundles are loaded unnecessarily
Use next/dynamic for code splitting heavy components
Check for waterfall requests: use parallel fetching or preload patterns
Verify images use next/image with proper sizes and priority props
Check if using 'use client' unnecessarily on large component trees

symptoms: build succeeds locally but fails in CI
root_cause: Environment differences between local and Vercel build environment
fix: Check Vercel build logs in deployment details for exact error
Compare Node.js versions: local vs Vercel (set in project settings)
Run 'next build' locally with same env vars to reproduce
Check for case-sensitive import paths (Vercel runs Linux, may differ from macOS)
Verify all env vars needed at build time are set in Vercel
Check for missing dependencies: move from devDependencies if needed at build
Clear .next folder and node_modules, then rebuild: rm -rf .next node_modules && npm install && npm run build

root_cause: Memory leak in serverless function or improper resource cleanup
fix: Check Vercel Function logs for memory usage patterns
Ensure database connections are properly closed or pooled
Avoid storing large objects in module-level variables (persists across warm invocations)
Profile locally with --inspect flag: node --inspect node_modules/.bin/next dev
Check for event listener leaks: always remove listeners in useEffect cleanup
Use streaming for large responses instead of buffering entire payload

root_cause: CSS-in-JS library not configured for server-side rendering or font loading not optimized
fix: For Tailwind: ensure globals.css is imported in root layout.tsx
For CSS-in-JS (styled-components): configure compiler in next.config.js: compiler: { styledComponents: true }
Use next/font for optimized font loading: import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'
Add font-display: swap for custom fonts
Preload critical CSS with <link rel='preload'>

root_cause: Cookie configuration incompatible with Vercel deployment domain or SameSite policy
fix: Set cookie domain to match production domain (omit for current domain only)
Use SameSite: 'lax' for most cases, 'none' requires Secure flag
Ensure cookies are set with Secure: true in production (HTTPS)
For cross-subdomain auth: set domain to '.yourdomain.com'
Check that cookie path is '/' for site-wide access
Verify cookie is not being blocked by browser privacy settings

root_cause: useEffect dependency array causes infinite loop or object/array reference changes on every render
fix: Check useEffect dependencies: avoid objects/arrays that create new references each render
Memoize objects with useMemo and functions with useCallback
Move static objects outside component or use useRef for stable references
Split useEffect into multiple focused effects with minimal dependencies
Use primitive values in dependency arrays when possible

root_cause: Unoptimized bundle with unnecessary client-side JavaScript
fix: Analyze bundle: npm install @next/bundle-analyzer && add to next.config.js
Use dynamic imports for heavy components: const Heavy = dynamic(() => import('./Heavy'), { ssr: false })
Keep components as Server Components by default; only add 'use client' when needed
Tree-shake large libraries: import { specific } from 'library' instead of import library
Use next/script with strategy='lazyOnload' for third-party scripts
Enable gzip/brotli compression (Vercel handles this automatically)

root_cause: Unoptimized database access pattern in serverless environment
fix: Enable connection pooling (PgBouncer, Prisma Accelerate, or Vercel Postgres)
Add database indexes for frequently queried columns
Implement pagination instead of fetching all records
Use select to fetch only needed fields: prisma.user.findMany({ select: { id: true, name: true } })
Cache frequent queries with unstable_cache or React cache()
Place database in same region as Vercel serverless functions (check in Vercel dashboard)
Use Vercel Edge Config for frequently read, rarely written configuration data

root_cause: Images not optimized or missing proper sizing configuration
fix: Use next/image component for automatic optimization
Add priority prop to LCP image: <Image priority src={...} />
Always specify width and height or use fill with sizes prop
Use sizes prop for responsive images: sizes='(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw'
Serve modern formats (WebP/AVIF) - next/image handles this automatically
Use placeholder='blur' with blurDataURL for perceived performance
Configure remote patterns for external image sources in next.config.js

root_cause: Large serverless function bundle size or heavy initialization code
fix: Minimize function bundle: avoid importing entire libraries when only using one function
Use edge runtime for latency-sensitive routes: export const runtime = 'edge'
Reduce dependency count in API routes
Pre-warm functions with Vercel Cron hitting critical paths
Choose function region closest to your users in vercel.json: { "regions": ["iad1"] }
Split large API routes into smaller, focused route handlers

root_cause: tsconfig.json strict mode enabled without proper type annotations throughout codebase
fix: Enable strict incrementally: start with 'noImplicitAny': true, then add stricter options
Use ts-expect-error for temporary suppressions during migration
Generate proper types from Prisma schema: npx prisma generate
Use zod for runtime validation with TypeScript inference: z.infer<typeof schema>

root_cause: Path aliases configured in tsconfig.json but not matching Next.js resolution
fix: Ensure tsconfig.json has: { "compilerOptions": { "baseUrl": ".", "paths": { "@/*": ["./src/*"] } } }
Next.js automatically reads tsconfig paths - no extra webpack config needed
Verify baseUrl matches your project structure
Restart TypeScript server after tsconfig changes: Cmd+Shift+P > Restart TS Server

symptoms: TypeScript errors not showing in VS Code
type checking passes but runtime fails
root_cause: TypeScript version mismatch between VS Code and project, or stale generated types
fix: Use workspace TypeScript version: VS Code > Cmd+Shift+P > Select TypeScript Version > Use Workspace Version
Regenerate Prisma types: npx prisma generate
Restart TS server after installing new @types packages
Clear .next folder: rm -rf .next && npm run dev
Ensure next-env.d.ts is not gitignored

symptoms: how to type API route handlers
TypeScript types for Next.js route handlers
root_cause: Need proper TypeScript typing for App Router route handlers
fix: Import types: import { NextRequest, NextResponse } from 'next/server'
Type route handler: export async function GET(request: NextRequest, { params }: { params: { id: string } }) {}
Use generics for response: NextResponse.json<ResponseType>(data)
Create shared type definitions in lib/types.ts for request/response shapes
Use zod for runtime request body validation with TypeScript inference

root_cause: Need to properly type async Server Components with params and searchParams
fix: Type page props: export default async function Page({ params, searchParams }: { params: { slug: string }; searchParams: { q?: string } }) {}
For Next.js 15+: params and searchParams are Promises - await them
Use satisfies for layout metadata: export const metadata = { title: 'Page' } satisfies Metadata
Define reusable types: type PageProps = { params: Promise<{ id: string }> }

symptoms: how to setup Next.js TypeScript project from scratch
root_cause: Need a properly configured Next.js TypeScript development environment
fix: Create project: npx create-next-app@latest --typescript --tailwind --eslint --app --src-dir
Key tsconfig options: strict: true, forceConsistentCasingInFileNames: true, noUncheckedIndexedAccess: true
Install dev dependencies: npm i -D @types/node prettier eslint-config-prettier
Add scripts: "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit", "lint": "next lint"
Configure .env.local for local development environment variables

symptoms: how to use SQLite locally with Turso in production
root_cause: Need SQLite for fast local development with Turso (libSQL) as production database on Vercel
fix: Install: npm install @libsql/client
Local config: createClient({ url: 'file:local.db' })
Production config: createClient({ url: process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!, authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN! })
Use conditional: const db = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? localClient : tursoClient
Add local.db to .gitignore

root_cause: Turso environment variables not set correctly or using wrong URL format for Vercel
fix: Use libsql:// protocol for remote Turso: TURSO_DATABASE_URL=libsql://your-db-name-org.turso.io
Generate auth token: turso db tokens create your-db-name
Set both TURSO_DATABASE_URL and TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN in Vercel environment variables
For embedded replicas on Vercel, use the HTTP URL: https://your-db-name-org.turso.io
Verify token hasn't expired: turso db tokens create your-db-name --expiration none

symptoms: Drizzle ORM with SQLite and Turso
root_cause: Need type-safe ORM layer for SQLite/Turso with good developer experience
fix: Install: npm install drizzle-orm @libsql/client && npm install -D drizzle-kit
Define schema: export const users = sqliteTable('users', { id: integer('id').primaryKey(), name: text('name').notNull() })
Create client: import { drizzle } from 'drizzle-orm/libsql'; const db = drizzle(client)
drizzle.config.ts: export default { schema: './src/db/schema.ts', driver: 'turso', dbCredentials: { url: process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!, authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN } }
Migrate: npx drizzle-kit push:sqlite

root_cause: SQLite does not support concurrent writes; multiple dev server requests causing lock contention
fix: Enable WAL mode: PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL
Set busy timeout: PRAGMA busy_timeout=5000
Use a single database connection instance (singleton pattern)
For heavy concurrency needs, switch to Turso which handles this at the edge
Avoid long-running transactions in development

root_cause: Need low-latency reads with Turso embedded replicas for edge/serverless
fix: Install: npm install @libsql/client
Create synced client: createClient({ url: 'file:replica.db', syncUrl: process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL, authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN, syncInterval: 60 })
Writes go to remote, reads from local replica
Call client.sync() to manually trigger sync
Note: Embedded replicas not supported on Vercel serverless (no persistent filesystem) - use for non-serverless deployments

symptoms: schema drift between local and production
root_cause: Database migrations not properly applied across environments
fix: Use Drizzle Kit for migrations: npx drizzle-kit generate:sqlite && npx drizzle-kit push:sqlite
Or Turso CLI: turso db shell your-db < migrations/001.sql
Compare schemas: npx drizzle-kit introspect:sqlite to see current remote state
Keep migration files in version control under /drizzle or /migrations
Test migrations locally on file:test.db before applying to production

symptoms: how to setup Prisma with Neon PostgreSQL
root_cause: Need to configure Prisma ORM with Neon's serverless PostgreSQL for Vercel deployment
fix: Install: npm install prisma @prisma/client @prisma/adapter-neon @neondatabase/serverless
Set DATABASE_URL in .env: postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx.region.neon.tech/dbname?sslmode=require
In schema.prisma: generator client { provider = 'prisma-client-js' previewFeatures = ['driverAdapters'] }

Initialize client with Neon adapter: import { Pool, neonConfig } from '@neondatabase/serverless'; const pool = new Pool({ connectionString: process.env.DATABASE_URL }); const adapter = new PrismaNeon(pool); const prisma = new PrismaClient({ adapter })
Generate client: npx prisma generate && npx prisma db push

root_cause: Serverless functions create too many connections without proper pooling configuration
fix: Use Neon's connection pooler URL (port 5432 for session mode, or use pooled connection string)
Add ?pgbouncer=true&connect_timeout=15 to connection string for pooled mode
Set connection_limit in Prisma schema: datasource db { url = env("DATABASE_URL") directUrl = env("DIRECT_URL") }
Use DIRECT_URL for migrations, DATABASE_URL (pooled) for runtime
Singleton pattern: globalThis.prisma = globalThis.prisma || new PrismaClient()

root_cause: Prisma Client needs to be regenerated after schema changes or in CI/build environment
fix: Run: npx prisma generate (locally and in build)
Add to build script: "build": "prisma generate && next build"
Or add postinstall script: "postinstall": "prisma generate"
For monorepos, ensure prisma generate runs in the correct package directory
Clear node_modules/.prisma and regenerate if types are stale

root_cause: Need efficient and type-safe database query patterns with Prisma
fix: Use select for partial queries: prisma.user.findMany({ select: { id: true, email: true } })
Use include for relations: prisma.post.findMany({ include: { author: true } })
Transaction: await prisma.$transaction([query1, query2]) or prisma.$transaction(async (tx) => {})
Pagination: prisma.post.findMany({ skip: page * limit, take: limit, orderBy: { createdAt: 'desc' } })
Use Prisma.validator for reusable query args: const userSelect = Prisma.validator<Prisma.UserSelect>()({ id: true, name: true })

symptoms: first query to Neon takes long
root_cause: Neon auto-suspends compute after inactivity, causing cold start on first query
fix: Use Neon's serverless driver (@neondatabase/serverless) for WebSocket connections - faster cold starts
Enable auto-scaling with minimum compute size to reduce suspend frequency
Use connection pooling to keep connections warm
Place Neon database in same region as Vercel functions (e.g., us-east-1 / iad1)
For critical paths, add a warmup query in middleware or cron
Consider Neon Pro plan for always-on compute endpoint

root_cause: Need safe migration workflow for Prisma with Neon branching
fix: Development: npx prisma migrate dev --name description (uses DIRECT_URL)
Production: npx prisma migrate deploy (in CI/CD pipeline)
Use Neon branching for safe testing: neon branches create --name feature-branch
Set DIRECT_URL to non-pooled Neon connection for migrations
Add to CI: npx prisma migrate deploy before deployment
Rollback: npx prisma migrate resolve --rolled-back migration_name

symptoms: how to setup Supabase realtime in Next.js
root_cause: Need real-time data synchronization between server and clients using Supabase Realtime
fix: Install: npm install @supabase/supabase-js @supabase/ssr
Create browser client: createBrowserClient(process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!, process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!)
Subscribe to changes: supabase.channel('room').on('postgres_changes', { event: '*', schema: 'public', table: 'messages' }, (payload) => { handleChange(payload) }).subscribe()
Enable realtime on table: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE messages
Unsubscribe on cleanup: supabase.removeChannel(channel)

symptoms: Supabase client in Next.js App Router
root_cause: Need separate Supabase clients for server components, client components, and middleware
fix: Create utils/supabase/server.ts: import { createServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr'; use cookies() from next/headers
Create utils/supabase/client.ts: import { createBrowserClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
Create utils/supabase/middleware.ts: for auth session refresh in middleware
Server component: const supabase = createServerClient(url, key, { cookies }); const { data } = await supabase.from('posts').select()
Client component: const supabase = createBrowserClient(url, key); use for realtime subscriptions

root_cause: Realtime not enabled on table, RLS blocking subscription, or client not properly subscribed
fix: Enable realtime: Go to Supabase Dashboard > Database > Replication > Enable for target table
Or SQL: ALTER PUBLICATION supabase_realtime ADD TABLE your_table
Check RLS policies allow SELECT for the subscribing user role
Ensure .subscribe() is called and status is 'SUBSCRIBED'
Check channel status: channel.on('system', { event: '*' }, (status) => console.log(status))
Verify NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY are correct

root_cause: Need to implement real-time messaging/notifications with proper subscription management
fix: Create channel: const channel = supabase.channel('chat-room')
Listen for inserts: channel.on('postgres_changes', { event: 'INSERT', schema: 'public', table: 'messages', filter: 'room_id=eq.123' }, (payload) => setMessages(prev => [...prev, payload.new]))
Subscribe: channel.subscribe()
Send message via server action or API: await supabase.from('messages').insert({ content, room_id, user_id })
Cleanup in useEffect return: () => { supabase.removeChannel(channel) }
Use Broadcast for ephemeral events (typing indicators): channel.send({ type: 'broadcast', event: 'typing', payload: { user_id } })

root_cause: Row Level Security policies not configured to allow access for the current user/role
fix: Check existing policies: SELECT * FROM pg_policies WHERE tablename = 'your_table'
Create SELECT policy: CREATE POLICY 'read_own' ON messages FOR SELECT USING (auth.uid() = user_id)
Create INSERT policy: CREATE POLICY 'insert_own' ON messages FOR INSERT WITH CHECK (auth.uid() = user_id)
For public read: CREATE POLICY 'public_read' ON posts FOR SELECT USING (true)
Service role key bypasses RLS - use only on server side, never expose to client
Test policies in Supabase Dashboard SQL editor with SET ROLE authenticated

root_cause: Each client creates a WebSocket connection; concurrent users can exceed plan limits
fix: Consolidate subscriptions: use one channel with multiple event listeners instead of multiple channels
Unsubscribe from channels when components unmount or pages change
Use Supabase Realtime filters to reduce payload: filter: 'room_id=eq.specific-room'
For high traffic, use Broadcast instead of Postgres Changes (lower latency, no DB trigger)
Monitor connection count in Supabase Dashboard > Realtime > Connections
Consider Supabase Pro plan for higher concurrent connection limits

root_cause: Need to track and display real-time user presence/online status
fix: Create presence channel: const channel = supabase.channel('online-users', { config: { presence: { key: userId } } })
Track presence: channel.on('presence', { event: 'sync' }, () => { const state = channel.presenceState(); setOnlineUsers(Object.keys(state)) })
Subscribe and track: channel.subscribe(async (status) => { if (status === 'SUBSCRIBED') { await channel.track({ user_id: userId, online_at: new Date().toISOString() }) } })
Untrack on leave: channel.untrack()
Listen for join/leave: channel.on('presence', { event: 'join' }, ({ newPresences }) => {})

symptoms: how to setup Upstash QStash in Next.js
root_cause: Need to process background tasks that exceed Vercel's function timeout using QStash message queue
fix: Install: npm install @upstash/qstash
Create QStash client: import { Client } from '@upstash/qstash'; const qstash = new Client({ token: process.env.QSTASH_TOKEN! })
Publish message: await qstash.publishJSON({ url: 'https://your-app.vercel.app/api/process', body: { taskId: '123' } })
Create receiver endpoint: app/api/process/route.ts that handles the background task
Verify signature: import { Receiver } from '@upstash/qstash'; const receiver = new Receiver({ currentSigningKey, nextSigningKey })
Set QSTASH_TOKEN, QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY, QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY in Vercel env vars

root_cause: Need to schedule tasks for future execution on Vercel serverless
fix: Delay delivery: await qstash.publishJSON({ url: endpoint, body: payload, delay: 300 }) // 5 minutes
Schedule with cron: await qstash.schedules.create({ destination: endpoint, cron: '0 9 * * 1' }) // every Monday 9am
Use Not-Before header: headers: { 'Upstash-Not-Before': Math.floor(Date.now() / 1000) + 3600 }
Cancel scheduled: await qstash.schedules.delete(scheduleId)
List active schedules: await qstash.schedules.list()

root_cause: Need to handle failed message delivery and implement retry logic with QStash
fix: Set retry count: await qstash.publishJSON({ url: endpoint, body: payload, retries: 3 })
Configure callback for completion notification: callback: 'https://your-app.vercel.app/api/qstash-callback'
Set failure callback: failureCallback: 'https://your-app.vercel.app/api/qstash-dlq'
Your endpoint must return 2xx status to acknowledge - any other status triggers retry
View failed messages in Upstash Console > QStash > Dead Letter Queue
Replay DLQ messages: await qstash.dlq.deleteMany({ filter: { messageId } })

symptoms: how to setup Inngest with Next.js
root_cause: Need durable, event-driven background functions with built-in retry, scheduling, and observability
fix: Install: npm install inngest
Create client: import { Inngest } from 'inngest'; export const inngest = new Inngest({ id: 'my-app' })

Define function: export const processOrder = inngest.createFunction({ id: 'process-order' }, { event: 'order/created' }, async ({ event, step }) => { await step.run('charge', () => chargePayment(event.data)) })
Create serve endpoint: app/api/inngest/route.ts: import { serve } from 'inngest/next'; export const { GET, POST, PUT } = serve({ client: inngest, functions: [processOrder] })
Send event: await inngest.send({ name: 'order/created', data: { orderId: '123' } })
Set INNGEST_EVENT_KEY and INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY in Vercel env vars

root_cause: Need to orchestrate multi-step workflows with automatic retries and state persistence
fix: Define steps: async ({ step }) => { const user = await step.run('get-user', () => getUser(id)); const email = await step.run('send-email', () => sendEmail(user.email)); return { sent: true } }
Sleep between steps: await step.sleep('wait-1-hour', '1h')
Wait for event: const approval = await step.waitForEvent('wait-approval', { event: 'order/approved', timeout: '24h' })
Parallel steps: const [a, b] = await Promise.all([step.run('a', fnA), step.run('b', fnB)])
Each step retries independently on failure - no re-execution of completed steps

root_cause: Inngest serve endpoint not properly configured or dev server not synced
fix: Run Inngest dev server: npx inngest-cli@latest dev
Ensure serve route exports GET, POST, PUT: export const { GET, POST, PUT } = serve({ client, functions })
Dev server auto-discovers at http://localhost:3000/api/inngest - verify URL matches
Check that all functions are passed to serve() functions array
In production: sync via Vercel integration or manually at Inngest dashboard
Verify event name matches exactly between send() and createFunction trigger

root_cause: Need to process multiple items in parallel with individual retry handling
fix: Fan-out: inngest.createFunction({ id: 'process-batch' }, { event: 'batch/ready' }, async ({ event, step }) => { const items = event.data.items; for (const item of items) { await step.run(`process-${item.id}`, () => processItem(item)) } })
Or send individual events: await step.run('fan-out', () => inngest.send(items.map(item => ({ name: 'item/process', data: item }))))
Use step.sendEvent for fan-out within a function
Each step.run has independent retries - one failure doesn't block others
Use concurrency controls: { concurrency: { limit: 10 } } in function config

symptoms: when to use QStash vs Inngest
root_cause: Need to choose between QStash and Inngest for background processing on Vercel
fix: Use QStash for: simple HTTP callbacks, delayed delivery, one-off scheduled messages, lightweight queue needs
Use Inngest for: multi-step workflows, event-driven architectures, complex retry logic, long-running processes with sleep/wait
QStash is lower-level (HTTP message delivery), Inngest is higher-level (durable function orchestration)
They can complement each other: QStash for simple triggers, Inngest for complex workflows
Both work on Vercel's serverless model without dedicated infrastructure

root_cause: Tailwind content paths not configured to scan the correct directories
fix: Check tailwind.config.ts content paths include all component directories: content: ['./src/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}', './app/**/*.{js,ts,jsx,tsx,mdx}']
Ensure globals.css has @tailwind directives: @tailwind base; @tailwind components; @tailwind utilities;
Import globals.css in root layout.tsx: import './globals.css'
For Tailwind v4: use @import 'tailwindcss' in CSS file instead of directives
Restart dev server after tailwind.config changes

root_cause: Dark mode strategy not configured or class not being applied to html/body element
fix: Set darkMode in tailwind.config: darkMode: 'class' (for manual toggle) or 'media' (system preference)
Apply dark class to <html> element: <html className={theme === 'dark' ? 'dark' : ''}>
Use next-themes for easy toggle: npm install next-themes, wrap app in <ThemeProvider attribute='class'>
Use dark: prefix in classes: className='bg-white dark:bg-gray-900 text-black dark:text-white'
Suppress hydration warning on html element when using next-themes: suppressHydrationWarning

root_cause: Need responsive layouts using Tailwind's mobile-first breakpoint system
fix: Mobile-first: base styles are mobile, add breakpoints for larger: className='text-sm md:text-base lg:text-lg'
Responsive grid: className='grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-4'
Hide/show at breakpoints: className='hidden md:block' or 'block md:hidden'
Responsive padding: className='px-4 md:px-8 lg:px-16'
Custom breakpoints in config: theme: { screens: { xs: '475px', ...defaultTheme.screens } }

symptoms: custom colors and fonts in Tailwind
root_cause: Need to customize Tailwind with project-specific design tokens
fix: Extend theme in tailwind.config.ts: theme: { extend: { colors: { brand: { 50: '#f0f9ff', 500: '#0ea5e9', 900: '#0c4a6e' } } } }
Custom fonts: theme: { extend: { fontFamily: { sans: ['var(--font-inter)', ...defaultTheme.fontFamily.sans] } } }
Use with next/font: const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'], variable: '--font-inter' })
CSS variables for dynamic theming: extend: { colors: { primary: 'hsl(var(--primary))' } }
Add custom animations: extend: { keyframes: { fadeIn: { '0%': { opacity: '0' }, '100%': { opacity: '1' } } }, animation: { fadeIn: 'fadeIn 0.5s ease-in-out' } }

root_cause: Tailwind content scanning including unnecessary files or not purging correctly
fix: Tailwind v3+ purges by default in production - ensure content paths are correct and minimal
Don't use string concatenation for dynamic classes: use clsx or tailwind-merge instead
Bad: className={`text-${color}-500`} - Tailwind can't detect this at build time
Good: className={clsx({ 'text-red-500': color === 'red', 'text-blue-500': color === 'blue' })}
Use tailwind-merge to resolve conflicting classes: import { twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge'; twMerge('px-4 px-8') → 'px-8'
Install: npm install clsx tailwind-merge

root_cause: Need reusable component styling with variant support using Tailwind
fix: Use class-variance-authority (CVA): npm install class-variance-authority

Define variants: const button = cva('rounded font-medium', { variants: { intent: { primary: 'bg-blue-600 text-white', secondary: 'bg-gray-200 text-gray-800' }, size: { sm: 'px-2 py-1 text-sm', md: 'px-4 py-2', lg: 'px-6 py-3 text-lg' } }, defaultVariants: { intent: 'primary', size: 'md' } })
Use in component: <button className={button({ intent: 'primary', size: 'lg' })}>
Combine with tailwind-merge for override support: className={twMerge(button({ intent }), className)}
Type props with VariantProps: type ButtonProps = VariantProps<typeof button>

root_cause: CSS specificity conflicts or class order issues when merging Tailwind classes
fix: Use tailwind-merge to resolve conflicts: npm install tailwind-merge
Create utility: import { twMerge } from 'tailwind-merge'; import { clsx, type ClassValue } from 'clsx'; export function cn(...inputs: ClassValue[]) { return twMerge(clsx(inputs)) }
Use cn() for conditional + merged classes: className={cn('base-styles', conditional && 'extra', props.className)}
This resolves: cn('px-4', 'px-8') → 'px-8' (last wins)
Add !important with Tailwind: className='!text-red-500' (use sparingly)

symptoms: how to setup Redis with Upstash on Vercel
root_cause: Need a serverless-compatible Redis instance for caching, rate limiting, and session storage on Vercel
fix: Install: npm install @upstash/redis
Create client: import { Redis } from '@upstash/redis'; export const redis = Redis.fromEnv()
Set environment variables: UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL and UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_TOKEN in Vercel
For local dev: add same vars to .env.local (get from Upstash console)
Uses HTTP/REST protocol - no persistent connections needed (ideal for serverless)
Alternative: use Vercel KV (powered by Upstash) for tighter Vercel integration

root_cause: Need to prevent duplicate event/request processing in distributed serverless environment
fix: Generate dedup key from event content: const dedupKey = `dedup:${crypto.createHash('sha256').update(JSON.stringify(event)).digest('hex')}`
Check and set atomically: const exists = await redis.set(dedupKey, '1', { nx: true, ex: 3600 })
If exists is null, event was already processed - skip: if (!exists) return { deduplicated: true }
For webhook dedup: use webhook delivery ID as key: `dedup:webhook:${deliveryId}`
Set appropriate TTL based on dedup window: ex: 86400 for 24h dedup window

Clean pattern: async function isDuplicate(id: string, ttlSeconds = 3600): Promise<boolean> { const result = await redis.set(`dedup:${id}`, Date.now(), { nx: true, ex: ttlSeconds }); return result === null }

root_cause: Need distributed rate limiting across serverless function instances
fix: Use @upstash/ratelimit for production-ready implementation: npm install @upstash/ratelimit
Sliding window: const ratelimit = new Ratelimit({ redis, limiter: Ratelimit.slidingWindow(10, '10s') })
Token bucket: const ratelimit = new Ratelimit({ redis, limiter: Ratelimit.tokenBucket(10, '10s', 10) })
Fixed window: const ratelimit = new Ratelimit({ redis, limiter: Ratelimit.fixedWindow(100, '1h') })
Check: const { success, limit, remaining, reset } = await ratelimit.limit(identifier)
Add response headers: headers.set('X-RateLimit-Limit', limit); headers.set('X-RateLimit-Remaining', remaining)
Use in middleware for global rate limiting or in individual API routes

root_cause: Embedding API calls are expensive and slow; caching prevents redundant computation
fix: Hash input text for cache key: const cacheKey = `embed:${crypto.createHash('sha256').update(text).digest('hex')}`
Check cache first: const cached = await redis.get<number[]>(cacheKey); if (cached) return cached
Store after computing: await redis.set(cacheKey, embedding, { ex: 86400 * 7 }) // 7 day TTL
For batch: use redis.mget() and redis.mset() for multiple embeddings
Pipeline for efficiency: const pipe = redis.pipeline(); texts.forEach(t => pipe.get(`embed:${hash(t)}`)); const results = await pipe.exec()
Consider memory: 1536-dim float32 embedding ≈ 6KB; plan TTL and eviction accordingly

root_cause: RAG retrieval and LLM calls are expensive; caching similar queries reduces cost and latency
fix: Cache by query hash: const cacheKey = `rag:${crypto.createHash('sha256').update(query.toLowerCase().trim()).digest('hex')}`
Store retrieval results: await redis.set(cacheKey, JSON.stringify({ chunks, answer, timestamp: Date.now() }), { ex: 3600 })
Check cache before retrieval: const cached = await redis.get(cacheKey); if (cached) return JSON.parse(cached)
For semantic caching (similar but not exact queries): use Upstash Vector for similarity-based cache lookup
Invalidate on knowledge base update: await redis.del(...keys) or use key prefix with scan: const keys = await redis.keys('rag:*')
Set shorter TTL for time-sensitive data, longer for stable knowledge base content

root_cause: Need shared session state across serverless function instances on Vercel
fix: Store session data: await redis.set(`session:${sessionId}`, JSON.stringify(sessionData), { ex: 86400 })
Retrieve session: const session = await redis.get<SessionData>(`session:${sessionId}`)
Generate session ID: const sessionId = crypto.randomUUID()
Set session cookie: cookies().set('session_id', sessionId, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax', maxAge: 86400 })
Extend session on activity: await redis.expire(`session:${sessionId}`, 86400)
Delete on logout: await redis.del(`session:${sessionId}`); cookies().delete('session_id')
For auth sessions with NextAuth: configure Upstash Redis adapter

root_cause: Need to buffer incoming events and process them in batches to reduce database writes
fix: Push events to Redis list: await redis.lpush('event-buffer:analytics', JSON.stringify(event))
Set buffer size limit: if (await redis.llen('event-buffer:analytics') >= 100) { await flushBuffer() }
Flush with atomic pop: const events = await redis.lrange('event-buffer:analytics', 0, 99); await redis.ltrim('event-buffer:analytics', 100, -1)
Use sorted set for time-ordered buffer: await redis.zadd('event-buffer', { score: Date.now(), member: JSON.stringify(event) })
Flush periodically via cron/QStash: schedule /api/flush-buffer every 5 minutes
Alternative: use Redis Stream: await redis.xadd('events', '*', event) for ordered event log

root_cause: Need to track retry attempts and implement backoff logic for failed operations
fix: Track retry count: const retryKey = `retry:${jobId}`; const attempts = await redis.incr(retryKey)
Set max retries with TTL: await redis.expire(retryKey, 86400) // auto-cleanup after 24h
Check before retry: if (attempts > maxRetries) { await moveToDeadLetter(job); return }
Calculate backoff: const delay = Math.min(Math.pow(2, attempts) * 1000, 300000) // max 5 min
Store retry metadata: await redis.hset(`retry-meta:${jobId}`, { attempts, lastError: error.message, lastAttempt: Date.now(), nextRetry: Date.now() + delay })
Schedule retry with QStash delay: await qstash.publishJSON({ url: endpoint, body: job, delay: delay / 1000 })
Clean up on success: await redis.del(`retry:${jobId}`, `retry-meta:${jobId}`)

root_cause: Need efficient cache-aside pattern for database query results
fix: Implement cache-aside: async function getCached<T>(key: string, fetcher: () => Promise<T>, ttl = 3600): Promise<T> { const cached = await redis.get<T>(key); if (cached) return cached; const fresh = await fetcher(); await redis.set(key, fresh, { ex: ttl }); return fresh; }
Use with database queries: const users = await getCached('users:all', () => prisma.user.findMany(), 300)
Invalidate on mutation: await redis.del('users:all') // in server action after write
Use tags for group invalidation: store keys in a set: await redis.sadd('tag:users', cacheKey)
Invalidate by tag: const keys = await redis.smembers('tag:users'); if (keys.length) await redis.del(...keys, 'tag:users')

root_cause: Upstash Redis REST URL or token misconfigured, or network issue in serverless environment
fix: Verify UPSTASH_REDIS_REST_URL starts with https:// (not redis://)
Upstash uses REST API over HTTP - no TCP connections needed
Check token hasn't been rotated: regenerate in Upstash console if needed
Add retry logic: Redis.fromEnv() has automatic retries; configure with { automaticDeserialization: true }
For edge runtime: Upstash REST client works natively (no Node.js TCP needed)
Test connection: const result = await redis.ping(); // should return 'PONG'

root_cause: Need optimized local development setup with proper tooling for Next.js TypeScript project
fix: Start dev server: npm run dev (uses Turbopack in Next.js 14+: next dev --turbo)
Enable Turbopack for faster HMR: next dev --turbopack
Configure .env.local for local database URLs and API keys
Use SQLite locally, Turso/Neon in production for faster iteration
Install recommended VS Code extensions: Tailwind IntelliSense, Prisma, ESLint, Pretty TypeScript Errors

root_cause: ESLint configuration not properly set up for Next.js + TypeScript + Tailwind
fix: Use Next.js built-in: extends: ['next/core-web-vitals', 'next/typescript']
Add Prettier compatibility: npm i -D prettier eslint-config-prettier && add 'prettier' to extends array last
For strict: add '@typescript-eslint/recommended-type-checked' (requires parserOptions.project)
Tailwind class sorting: npm i -D prettier-plugin-tailwindcss (add to prettier config plugins)
Ignore generated files: add '.next', 'node_modules', 'prisma/generated' to .eslintignore

root_cause: Need runtime validation that integrates with TypeScript type system for forms and API inputs
fix: Install: npm install zod
Define schema: const CreateUserSchema = z.object({ email: z.string().email(), name: z.string().min(2), age: z.number().min(18).optional() })
Infer type: type CreateUser = z.infer<typeof CreateUserSchema>
Validate in server action: const result = CreateUserSchema.safeParse(formData); if (!result.success) return { errors: result.error.flatten().fieldErrors }
Validate API body: const body = CreateUserSchema.parse(await request.json()) // throws ZodError
Use with react-hook-form: npm install @hookform/resolvers; resolver: zodResolver(CreateUserSchema)

symptoms: TypeScript error: Type 'X' is not assignable to type 'Y'
root_cause: TypeScript strict mode catching type mismatches in component props or API responses
fix: For API responses: define explicit return types or use zod inference
For dynamic data: use type guards: function isUser(data: unknown): data is User { return typeof data === 'object' && data !== null && 'id' in data }
For union types: use discriminated unions with a 'type' field
For generic components: use satisfies operator: const config = { ... } satisfies Config
For third-party types: use module augmentation or declaration merging in .d.ts files

symptoms: shared types between client and server
root_cause: Need type safety across the client-server boundary without code duplication
fix: Define shared types in lib/types.ts: export type ApiResponse<T> = { data: T; error: null } | { data: null; error: string }
Use zod schemas as single source of truth: export const UserSchema = z.object({...}); export type User = z.infer<typeof UserSchema>
Type fetch wrapper: async function api<T>(url: string): Promise<ApiResponse<T>> { ... }
For server actions: return typed results that client components can consume
Use Prisma generated types for database entities: import { User, Post } from '@prisma/client'

symptoms: setup Prisma with local PostgreSQL and Neon production
root_cause: Need local PostgreSQL for development speed with Neon for production on Vercel
fix: schema.prisma: datasource db { provider = 'postgresql' url = env('DATABASE_URL') directUrl = env('DIRECT_URL') }
.env.local (development): DATABASE_URL='postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/myapp' DIRECT_URL='postgresql://postgres:postgres@localhost:5432/myapp'
.env.production (Vercel): DATABASE_URL='postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx.neon.tech/db?sslmode=require&pgbouncer=true' DIRECT_URL='postgresql://user:pass@ep-xxx.neon.tech/db?sslmode=require'
Local PostgreSQL with Docker: docker run -d --name postgres -e POSTGRES_PASSWORD=postgres -p 5432:5432 postgres:16
Migrate both: npx prisma migrate dev (local) and npx prisma migrate deploy (CI/production)

root_cause: Supabase auth flow requires proper cookie handling and callback URL configuration
fix: Verify auth callback route exists: app/auth/callback/route.ts

Callback must exchange code for session: const { searchParams } = new URL(request.url); const code = searchParams.get('code'); if (code) { const supabase = createServerClient(...); await supabase.auth.exchangeCodeForSession(code) }
Ensure middleware refreshes session: call supabase.auth.getUser() in middleware to refresh expired tokens
Check Supabase Dashboard > Auth > URL Configuration for redirect URLs
Add localhost:3000 to allowed redirect URLs for local development
Verify cookies are being set: check browser DevTools > Application > Cookies

root_cause: Database queries not optimized with proper indexes or query structure
fix: Add indexes for filtered columns: CREATE INDEX idx_messages_room_id ON messages(room_id)
Use compound indexes for common query patterns: CREATE INDEX idx_posts_user_created ON posts(user_id, created_at DESC)
Check query plan: EXPLAIN ANALYZE SELECT * FROM messages WHERE room_id = '...'
Use Supabase Dashboard > Database > Query Performance for slow query identification
Paginate with cursor-based pagination: .from('posts').select().lt('created_at', cursor).order('created_at', { ascending: false }).limit(20)
Use select() to fetch only needed columns: .select('id, title, created_at') instead of .select('*')

root_cause: Need server-side logic executed in database for performance or security
fix: Create function in SQL: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION get_nearby_users(lat float, lng float) RETURNS SETOF users AS $$ SELECT * FROM users WHERE earth_distance(ll_to_earth(lat, lng), ll_to_earth(users.lat, users.lng)) < 1000 $$ LANGUAGE sql
Call via client: const { data } = await supabase.rpc('get_nearby_users', { lat: 40.7, lng: -74.0 })
TypeScript types: generate with supabase gen types typescript --project-id your-project > database.types.ts
Use for complex logic that would require multiple round-trips from application
Functions respect RLS by default; use SECURITY DEFINER to bypass (carefully)

root_cause: Need to handle file uploads through Supabase Storage with proper access control
fix: Upload: const { data, error } = await supabase.storage.from('avatars').upload(`${userId}/${file.name}`, file, { cacheControl: '3600', upsert: true })
Get public URL: const { data: { publicUrl } } = supabase.storage.from('avatars').getPublicUrl('path/to/file')
Create signed URL: const { data } = await supabase.storage.from('private').createSignedUrl('path', 3600)
Set bucket policies in Dashboard or SQL for access control
From server action: use supabase.storage with service role for elevated access
Max file size: configure per bucket in Supabase Dashboard (default 50MB)

symptoms: Supabase type errors after schema change
TypeScript types out of sync with Supabase
root_cause: Auto-generated TypeScript types not regenerated after database schema changes
fix: Regenerate types: npx supabase gen types typescript --project-id your-project-id > src/lib/database.types.ts
Add to scripts: "db:types": "supabase gen types typescript --project-id $PROJECT_ID > src/lib/database.types.ts"
Use generated types: import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types'; type Message = Database['public']['Tables']['messages']['Row']
Pass to client: createClient<Database>(url, key)
Run after every migration: npx supabase db push && npm run db:types
For local dev: npx supabase gen types typescript --local > src/lib/database.types.ts

root_cause: Need to prevent multiple serverless function instances from executing the same critical section concurrently
fix: Acquire lock: const lockKey = `lock:${resource}`; const acquired = await redis.set(lockKey, instanceId, { nx: true, ex: 30 })
If not acquired, another instance holds it: if (!acquired) return { error: 'Resource locked' }
Release after work: await redis.eval('if redis.call("get",KEYS[1]) == ARGV[1] then return redis.call("del",KEYS[1]) else return 0 end', [lockKey], [instanceId])
Use Lua script to ensure only the lock owner can release
Set reasonable TTL to prevent deadlocks if instance crashes: ex: 30 (seconds)
For longer tasks, implement lock renewal: setInterval(() => redis.expire(lockKey, 30), 10000)

root_cause: Need lightweight pub/sub for event broadcasting without full WebSocket infrastructure
fix: Note: Traditional Redis pub/sub requires persistent connections - not ideal for serverless
For serverless: use Redis Streams or polling instead of pub/sub
Publish event: await redis.xadd('notifications:user123', '*', { type: 'message', data: JSON.stringify(payload) })
Read events: await redis.xrange('notifications:user123', lastId || '0', '+', 10)
For real-time in browser: use Supabase Realtime or Server-Sent Events with polling
Alternative: use QStash to fan-out events to multiple endpoints

root_cause: Multiple sequential Redis commands cause accumulated network latency in serverless
fix: Use pipeline for multiple commands: const pipe = redis.pipeline(); pipe.get('key1'); pipe.set('key2', 'val'); pipe.incr('counter'); const results = await pipe.exec()
Pipeline sends all commands in single HTTP request (with Upstash REST)
Batch cache reads: const pipe = redis.pipeline(); keys.forEach(k => pipe.get(k)); const values = await pipe.exec()
Use multi/exec for atomic transactions: const tx = redis.multi(); tx.incr('counter'); tx.lpush('log', entry); const results = await tx.exec()
Reduces N round trips to 1 - critical for serverless latency
Pipeline limit: up to 1000 commands per pipeline on Upstash

root_cause: Multiple requests simultaneously find expired cache key and all hit the database
fix: Implement cache stampede protection with probabilistic early expiry
Use lock-based recomputation: only one instance refreshes, others wait or serve stale

Pattern: const stale = await redis.get(key); if (!stale) { const lock = await redis.set(`lock:${key}`, '1', { nx: true, ex: 10 }); if (lock) { const fresh = await fetchData(); await redis.set(key, fresh, { ex: ttl }); return fresh } else { await sleep(100); return redis.get(key) } }
Or use stale-while-revalidate: store with extra buffer TTL, recompute in background when near expiry
Set jittered TTL to prevent synchronized expiration: ex: ttl + Math.random() * ttl * 0.1

symptoms: Redis data structures for common use cases
when to use which Redis type
root_cause: Need to choose the right Redis data structure for different use cases
fix: String (GET/SET): simple cache, counters, locks, feature flags, session data
Hash (HSET/HGET): user profiles, object storage, grouped counters (hgetall for full object)
List (LPUSH/LRANGE): event buffers, recent items, queues (FIFO with LPUSH/RPOP)
Set (SADD/SMEMBERS): unique collections, tags, dedup tracking, online users
Sorted Set (ZADD/ZRANGE): leaderboards, rate limiting windows, priority queues, time-series
Stream (XADD/XRANGE): event logs, message queues with consumer groups, audit trails

symptoms: Tailwind CSS not working after upgrade
root_cause: Tailwind v4 uses new CSS-based configuration and import syntax
fix: Tailwind v4 uses: @import 'tailwindcss' instead of @tailwind base/components/utilities
Configuration moves to CSS: @theme { --color-primary: #3b82f6; }
No more tailwind.config.js in v4 - all config in CSS with @theme directive
For Next.js: ensure PostCSS processes the new syntax correctly
Migration: npx @tailwindcss/upgrade to auto-migrate from v3 to v4
Stick with v3 if ecosystem (shadcn/ui, etc.) hasn't fully migrated

root_cause: Need polished animations and transitions for better user experience
fix: Basic transition: className='transition-all duration-300 ease-in-out hover:scale-105'
Fade in: className='animate-in fade-in duration-500' (with tailwindcss-animate plugin)
Skeleton loading: className='animate-pulse bg-gray-200 rounded h-4 w-full'
Slide in: extend keyframes in config or use: className='transition-transform translate-y-0' with conditional 'translate-y-full'
Install tailwindcss-animate: npm i tailwindcss-animate && add to plugins in tailwind.config
Group hover: className='group-hover:opacity-100 opacity-0 transition-opacity' on child with 'group' on parent

root_cause: Need to securely verify that incoming requests are from QStash and not spoofed
fix: Install: npm install @upstash/qstash

Create receiver: import { Receiver } from '@upstash/qstash'; const receiver = new Receiver({ currentSigningKey: process.env.QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY!, nextSigningKey: process.env.QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY! })
Verify in route handler: const body = await request.text(); const isValid = await receiver.verify({ signature: request.headers.get('upstash-signature')!, body })
Reject invalid: if (!isValid) return new Response('Invalid signature', { status: 401 })
Parse body after verification: const data = JSON.parse(body)
Set QSTASH_CURRENT_SIGNING_KEY and QSTASH_NEXT_SIGNING_KEY from Upstash console

root_cause: Need to control execution rate to avoid overwhelming external services or databases
fix: Set concurrency limit: inngest.createFunction({ id: 'send-email', concurrency: { limit: 5 } }, ...)
Throttle execution rate: inngest.createFunction({ id: 'api-call', throttle: { limit: 10, period: '1m' } }, ...)
Rate limit per key: throttle: { limit: 1, period: '1s', key: 'event.data.userId' }
Priority queue: priority: { run: 'event.data.priority' } // higher number = higher priority
Debounce: debounce: { period: '5s', key: 'event.data.userId' } // only last event in window executes
Use for API rate limits, email sending, webhook delivery

root_cause: Individual steps in Inngest functions have execution time limits based on hosting platform
fix: On Vercel: each step is limited by your function's maxDuration (10s hobby, 60s pro)
Break long operations into smaller steps: each step.run() is a separate serverless invocation
Stream large data processing across multiple steps: for (const batch of chunks) { await step.run(`process-${i}`, () => processBatch(batch)) }
Set maxDuration in route config: export const maxDuration = 60 (for the Inngest serve endpoint)
Use step.sleep() for delays instead of blocking: await step.sleep('wait', '30s')
Each step can retry independently without re-running completed steps

symptoms: SQLite vs PostgreSQL for local development
root_cause: Need to decide on database strategy that balances local development speed with production requirements
fix: SQLite locally + Turso production: best for simple schemas, fast local iteration, no Docker needed
PostgreSQL locally + Neon production: best when using PG-specific features (JSONB, full-text search, arrays)
Supabase local (supabase start) + Supabase cloud: best for realtime features, auth integration, storage
SQLite path: use @libsql/client or better-sqlite3 locally, Turso in production
PostgreSQL path: Docker locally, Neon serverless in production with Prisma ORM
Decision factors: need realtime? → Supabase. Simple CRUD? → SQLite/Turso. Complex queries? → PostgreSQL/Neon

root_cause: Need to control feature rollout and A/B testing without redeployment
fix: Store flags: await redis.hset('feature-flags', { 'new-ui': 'true', 'beta-chat': 'false', 'ai-enabled': '0.5' })
Check flag: const flags = await redis.hgetall<Record<string, string>>('feature-flags')
Percentage rollout: const enabled = parseFloat(flags['ai-enabled']) > Math.random()
User-specific: await redis.sismember('beta-users', userId) // Set-based targeting
Cache flags in request scope to avoid multiple Redis calls per page
Update without deploy: await redis.hset('feature-flags', { 'new-ui': 'false' })
Consider Vercel Edge Config for ultra-low-latency flag reads at edge

root_cause: Query filter doesn't match due to case sensitivity, type mismatch, or eventual consistency
fix: Check case sensitivity: PostgreSQL text comparison is case-sensitive by default
Use case-insensitive filter: prisma.user.findFirst({ where: { email: { equals: email, mode: 'insensitive' } } })
Verify ID types match: UUID strings vs auto-increment integers
After insert, use returned data instead of separate query: const user = await prisma.user.create({ data: {...} })
For Neon: serverless driver may have slight delay after write - use the same connection for read-after-write
Check if RLS (Supabase) or tenant filters are excluding the row

root_cause: Attempting to create/update a record that violates a unique constraint in the database
fix: Use upsert for create-or-update: await prisma.user.upsert({ where: { email }, create: { email, name }, update: { name } })

Handle error gracefully: try { await prisma.user.create({...}) } catch (e) { if (e instanceof Prisma.PrismaClientKnownRequestError && e.code === 'P2002') { return { error: 'Email already exists' } } throw e }
For soft-dedup: check existence first with findUnique before create
Identify which field: e.meta?.target contains the constraint field name
Use Redis dedup to prevent race conditions before DB write

root_cause: Need instant UI feedback while server action processes in background
fix: Use useOptimistic hook: const [optimisticMessages, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(messages, (state, newMsg) => [...state, { ...newMsg, pending: true }])
In form action: addOptimistic(newMessage); await sendMessage(formData)
Show pending state: className={message.pending ? 'opacity-50' : ''}
Revalidate after action: revalidatePath('/chat') to sync with server state
Handle errors: if server action fails, optimistic state is automatically rolled back on next revalidation
Combine with useTransition for loading states: const [isPending, startTransition] = useTransition()

root_cause: Need reliable, idempotent webhook processing with deduplication and retry tracking
fix: 1. Receive webhook → verify signature → extract delivery ID
2. Dedup check: const isDup = await redis.set(`webhook:${deliveryId}`, '1', { nx: true, ex: 86400 }); if (!isDup) return Response.json({ status: 'duplicate' })
3. Store payload: await redis.set(`webhook-payload:${deliveryId}`, JSON.stringify(payload), { ex: 86400 })
4. Queue for processing: await inngest.send({ name: 'webhook/received', data: { deliveryId, source: 'stripe' } })
5. Process in Inngest function with retry: const payload = await step.run('get-payload', () => redis.get(`webhook-payload:${deliveryId}`))
6. Return 200 immediately to webhook sender, process async
7. Track status: await redis.hset(`webhook-status:${deliveryId}`, { status: 'processed', processedAt: Date.now() })

root_cause: Need to compose multiple middleware concerns (auth, rate limiting, logging) in Next.js
fix: Single middleware.ts file handles all concerns in sequence

Pattern: export async function middleware(req: NextRequest) { const rateLimit = await checkRateLimit(req); if (!rateLimit.success) return Response.json({error:'Rate limited'}, {status:429}); const session = await validateSession(req); if (!session && isProtectedPath(req)) return NextResponse.redirect(new URL('/login', req.url)); return NextResponse.next() }
Use matcher to skip static assets: export const config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico).*)'] }
For heavy middleware logic: keep fast, defer complex work to API routes
Middleware runs on edge - ensure all imports are edge-compatible

root_cause: Database growing beyond expected size due to WAL, deleted data, or inefficient schema
fix: Check size: turso db show your-db-name
Vacuum database to reclaim space: VACUUM (removes deleted rows from file)
Check largest tables: SELECT name, SUM(pgsize) as size FROM dbstat GROUP BY name ORDER BY size DESC
Implement data retention: DELETE FROM logs WHERE created_at < datetime('now', '-30 days')
Use INTEGER instead of TEXT for IDs where possible (more compact)
Consider partitioning large tables or archiving old data
Turso free tier: 9GB total storage across all databases

root_cause: Need full-text search capability using SQLite FTS5 for local/Turso databases
fix: Create FTS table: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE posts_fts USING fts5(title, content, content=posts, content_rowid=id)
Populate: INSERT INTO posts_fts(rowid, title, content) SELECT id, title, content FROM posts
Search: SELECT posts.* FROM posts_fts JOIN posts ON posts.id = posts_fts.rowid WHERE posts_fts MATCH 'search term' ORDER BY rank
Auto-sync with triggers: CREATE TRIGGER posts_ai AFTER INSERT ON posts BEGIN INSERT INTO posts_fts(rowid, title, content) VALUES (new.id, new.title, new.content); END
Snippet highlighting: SELECT snippet(posts_fts, 1, '<mark>', '</mark>', '...', 32) as snippet FROM posts_fts WHERE posts_fts MATCH ?
Works on both local SQLite and Turso

symptoms: Inngest function not found in production
root_cause: Inngest serve endpoint not properly deployed or functions not included in serve() call
fix: Ensure all functions are exported and passed to serve: export const { GET, POST, PUT } = serve({ client: inngest, functions: [fn1, fn2, fn3] })
Check that the serve route is accessible: visit https://your-app.vercel.app/api/inngest
Sync manually from Inngest dashboard if auto-sync fails
Verify INNGEST_SIGNING_KEY is set in Vercel (not needed for dev, required for production)
Check Inngest dashboard > Apps for sync status and errors
For Vercel: install Inngest Vercel integration for automatic sync on deploy

symptoms: Tailwind CSS file large in development
root_cause: Tailwind development build includes all utilities; PostCSS processing can be slow on large projects
fix: Use Turbopack: next dev --turbopack for faster CSS processing
Minimize content paths: only include directories that actually use Tailwind classes
Split CSS: use CSS Modules for component-specific styles, Tailwind for utility classes
Production is always optimized: Tailwind v3+ only includes used classes in production build
For very large projects: consider using Tailwind's JIT engine (default in v3+)
Avoid @apply in most cases - it increases CSS size; use component composition instead

symptoms: Supabase Edge Functions vs Vercel Functions
root_cause: Need to decide between Supabase Edge Functions and Vercel Serverless Functions for backend logic
fix: Use Vercel Functions (Next.js API routes) for: page-related logic, SSR data fetching, user-facing API endpoints
Use Supabase Edge Functions for: database triggers, webhook handlers, background tasks close to data
Use Inngest/QStash for: long-running workflows, scheduled jobs, event-driven processing
Vercel functions have better Next.js integration (caching, revalidation, streaming)
Supabase functions have direct database access without network hop
Most Next.js apps should primarily use Vercel API routes and reserve Supabase functions for DB-adjacent tasks

root_cause: Need a real-time leaderboard or ranking system with efficient top-N queries
fix: Add score: await redis.zadd('leaderboard:weekly', { score: points, member: userId })
Get top 10: await redis.zrange('leaderboard:weekly', 0, 9, { rev: true, withScores: true })
Get user rank: await redis.zrevrank('leaderboard:weekly', userId)
Increment score: await redis.zincrby('leaderboard:weekly', incrementBy, userId)
Get user score: await redis.zscore('leaderboard:weekly', userId)
Reset weekly: await redis.del('leaderboard:weekly') // via cron/QStash
Combine with user data: fetch user details from DB after getting ranked IDs

root_cause: Parallel and intercepting routes have strict file naming conventions that must be followed exactly
fix: Parallel routes use @folder convention: app/@modal/page.tsx, app/layout.tsx must accept {modal} prop
Intercepting routes use (.) (..) (...) convention: app/@modal/(.)photo/[id]/page.tsx
Layout must render the slot: export default function Layout({ children, modal }) { return <>{children}{modal}</> }
Provide default.tsx for parallel route slots: app/@modal/default.tsx returning null
Hard navigation (full page refresh) resets intercepted routes - use Link for soft navigation
Check file structure carefully - one misnamed folder breaks the entire pattern

root_cause: Need to stream responses from LLM/AI APIs or long-running processes to the client
fix: Use ReadableStream: return new Response(new ReadableStream({ async start(controller) { for await (const chunk of aiStream) { controller.enqueue(new TextEncoder().encode(chunk)); } controller.close(); } }))
With AI SDK: import { streamText } from 'ai'; const result = await streamText({ model, messages }); return result.toDataStreamResponse()
Client consumption with AI SDK: const { messages, isLoading } = useChat({ api: '/api/chat' })
For SSE: set headers Content-Type: text/event-stream, Cache-Control: no-cache
Vercel supports streaming in both Node.js and Edge runtime
Set maxDuration for long streams: export const maxDuration = 60

symptoms: TypeScript build errors only in CI
type errors on Vercel but not locally
root_cause: Next.js runs type checking during build; local dev may skip strict checks
fix: Run typecheck locally before pushing: npx tsc --noEmit
Add to CI: "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit" and run before build
Check for different TypeScript versions: local vs CI (pin in package.json)
next build runs strict type checking by default - match this locally
Common culprits: unused variables (noUnusedLocals), implicit any, missing return types
To temporarily skip during build (not recommended): typescript: { ignoreBuildErrors: true } in next.config.js
Fix progressively: use // @ts-expect-error with explanation for known issues

root_cause: Need reliable email delivery with retry logic and rate limiting for transactional emails
fix: Define email function: inngest.createFunction({ id: 'send-email', throttle: { limit: 10, period: '1s' }, retries: 3 }, { event: 'email/send' }, async ({ event, step }) => { await step.run('send', () => sendEmail(event.data)) })
Queue email: await inngest.send({ name: 'email/send', data: { to: user.email, template: 'welcome', vars: { name: user.name } } })
Batch emails: await inngest.send(users.map(u => ({ name: 'email/send', data: { to: u.email, template: 'newsletter' } })))
Track delivery: await step.run('track', () => redis.hset(`email:${event.data.id}`, { status: 'sent', sentAt: Date.now() }))
Use Resend, SendGrid, or Postmark as email provider in the step.run

root_cause: Vercel preview deployments not connected to correct Neon database branch
fix: Use Neon-Vercel integration for automatic branch mapping
Integration auto-creates Neon branch for each Vercel preview deployment
Set DATABASE_URL per environment in Vercel: Production → main branch, Preview → feature branch
Without integration: manually create branch: neon branches create --name preview-branch
Run migrations on preview branch: npx prisma migrate deploy (with preview branch DATABASE_URL)
Delete unused branches: neon branches delete old-branch-name

root_cause: Need a single database utility that works across local dev, preview, and production environments
fix: Create lib/db.ts with environment detection: export function getDb() { if (process.env.DATABASE_PROVIDER === 'sqlite') return getSqliteClient(); if (process.env.DATABASE_PROVIDER === 'supabase') return getSupabaseClient(); return getPrismaClient(); }

For SQLite/Turso: const client = process.env.NODE_ENV === 'development' ? createClient({ url: 'file:local.db' }) : createClient({ url: process.env.TURSO_DATABASE_URL!, authToken: process.env.TURSO_AUTH_TOKEN! })
For Prisma/Neon: use globalThis singleton pattern
Set DATABASE_PROVIDER in .env.local and Vercel environment variables
Export typed helper functions that abstract the provider

root_cause: Need a simple reliable job queue when QStash/Inngest is overkill
fix: Enqueue job: await redis.lpush('queue:emails', JSON.stringify({ id: uuid(), type: 'welcome', data: { userId } }))
Dequeue (via cron/API): const job = await redis.rpop('queue:emails')
Reliable queue (process and ack): const job = await redis.rpoplpush('queue:emails', 'queue:emails:processing')
After processing: await redis.lrem('queue:emails:processing', 1, job)
Check queue length: const pending = await redis.llen('queue:emails')
Process via Vercel Cron: schedule /api/process-queue every minute
For complex needs, prefer QStash or Inngest over hand-rolled Redis queue

root_cause: Tailwind cannot detect dynamically constructed class names at build time
fix: Never construct classes dynamically: BAD: `bg-${color}-500` or `text-${size}`
Use complete class names with conditional logic: GOOD: color === 'red' ? 'bg-red-500' : 'bg-blue-500'
For dynamic values, use inline styles: style={{ backgroundColor: color }}
Safelist specific patterns if needed: safelist: ['bg-red-500', 'bg-blue-500'] in tailwind.config
Use cva (class-variance-authority) for variant-based styling
Map data to complete class names: const colorMap: Record<string, string> = { red: 'bg-red-500', blue: 'bg-blue-500' }

root_cause: Excessive serverless function invocations increasing cost and latency
fix: Use ISR/caching to serve static content without function invocation
Implement stale-while-revalidate: export const revalidate = 3600 // serve stale, refresh in background
Batch API calls: combine multiple small requests into single endpoint
Use Edge Config for frequently read config data (no function invocation)
Cache with Redis: check cache before expensive computation
Use Client Components for interactive features that don't need server data
Deduplicate with Redis: prevent processing same event multiple times

root_cause: Need proper testing setup for Next.js TypeScript project with various testing strategies
fix: Install: npm i -D vitest @testing-library/react @testing-library/jest-dom happy-dom
Configure vitest.config.ts: import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'; export default defineConfig({ test: { environment: 'happy-dom', globals: true, setupFiles: ['./tests/setup.ts'] } })
Test server actions: directly import and call with mocked dependencies
Test API routes: use the route handler function directly or fetch with test server
Database tests: use SQLite in-memory for fast unit tests, real DB for integration
Scripts: "test": "vitest run", "test:watch": "vitest"
For E2E: use Playwright - npm i -D @playwright/test

root_cause: Need time-windowed counters for analytics, usage tracking, or rate limiting
fix: Simple counter with TTL: const key = `counter:${userId}:${getHourBucket()}`; await redis.incr(key); await redis.expire(key, 7200)
Helper: function getHourBucket() { return Math.floor(Date.now() / 3600000) }
Get current count: const count = await redis.get<number>(key) ?? 0

Rolling window with sorted set: await redis.zadd(`hits:${endpoint}`, { score: Date.now(), member: `${Date.now()}-${Math.random()}` }); await redis.zremrangebyscore(`hits:${endpoint}`, 0, Date.now() - 60000); const count = await redis.zcard(`hits:${endpoint}`)
Daily unique visitors: await redis.pfadd(`uv:${today}`, visitorId); const estimate = await redis.pfcount(`uv:${today}`)
HyperLogLog for unique counts: uses ~12KB regardless of cardinality

root_cause: QStash target endpoint failing or timing out, messages accumulating in dead letter queue
fix: Check target endpoint is publicly accessible (not localhost)
Endpoint must return 2xx within timeout to acknowledge delivery
Increase endpoint timeout: export const maxDuration = 60 on the receiving route
Check QStash Dashboard > Logs for specific error responses
Handle gracefully: return 200 even if processing will be async (acknowledge receipt, process later)
Replay failed messages from DLQ after fixing: POST /v2/dlq/{messageId}/replay
Set proper retries and backoff: retries: 5, backoff: 'exponential'

root_cause: Need scheduled recurring tasks with Inngest instead of Vercel Cron for complex workflows
fix: Define cron function: inngest.createFunction({ id: 'daily-cleanup' }, { cron: '0 2 * * *' }, async ({ step }) => { const deleted = await step.run('cleanup-old-data', () => prisma.log.deleteMany({ where: { createdAt: { lt: thirtyDaysAgo } } })); await step.run('notify', () => sendSlackNotification(`Cleaned ${deleted.count} records`)); })
Multiple schedules: { cron: ['0 9 * * 1-5', '0 12 * * 6-7'] }
Use step.run for each atomic operation - automatic retry on failure
Monitor in Inngest dashboard: see execution history, duration, errors
Cron expressions in UTC by default

root_cause: Postgres Changes use WAL-based replication which adds latency compared to Broadcast
fix: Postgres Changes typically have 100-500ms latency (WAL → detect → broadcast)
For lower latency: use Broadcast channel for ephemeral events, only use postgres_changes for persistence
Pattern: INSERT to DB + simultaneously broadcast via channel for instant UI update
await supabase.from('messages').insert(msg); channel.send({ type: 'broadcast', event: 'new-message', payload: msg })
Listen to broadcast for instant update, use postgres_changes as fallback/consistency check
Reduce payload size: use filter to only subscribe to relevant rows

root_cause: Need to prevent duplicate request processing from network retries or double-clicks
fix: Client sends idempotency key: headers: { 'Idempotency-Key': crypto.randomUUID() }
Middleware checks: const idempotencyKey = request.headers.get('idempotency-key')
Check Redis: const existing = await redis.get(`idem:${idempotencyKey}`); if (existing) return Response.json(JSON.parse(existing))
Process request and cache response: const response = await processRequest(); await redis.set(`idem:${idempotencyKey}`, JSON.stringify(response), { ex: 86400 })
Return cached response for duplicate requests within TTL window
For forms: generate key on form mount, reuse on retry: useRef(crypto.randomUUID())

symptoms: high latency between function and database
root_cause: Vercel functions and database deployed in different geographic regions causing latency
fix: Set Vercel function region to match database: vercel.json: { "regions": ["iad1"] }
Or per-route: export const preferredRegion = 'iad1' in route.ts
Neon: create database in us-east-1 to match Vercel's default iad1 region
Turso: use multi-region replicas for global apps, or single region matching Vercel
Supabase: choose AWS region matching Vercel (us-east-1 = iad1)
Check current region in Vercel Dashboard > Settings > Functions
Latency difference: same region ~1-5ms, cross-region ~50-150ms
