User-consistent: hash(userId + featureName) % 100 < percentage * 100 (same user always gets same result)
Kill switch: await redis.hset("features", { newCheckout: "0" }) // instant disable, no redeploy
Monitor: track errors by feature flag state, auto-disable if error rate spikes
Cleanup: after 100% rollout for 1 week with no issues, remove flag check from code

root_cause: Generate PDF documents server-side for invoices, reports, or certificates
fix: Install: npm install @react-pdf/renderer (React-based PDF)
Create template: import { Document, Page, Text, View } from "@react-pdf/renderer"; function Invoice({ data }) { return <Document><Page><Text>{data.title}</Text></Page></Document> }
Generate in API route: import { renderToBuffer } from "@react-pdf/renderer"; const buffer = await renderToBuffer(<Invoice data={data} />)
Return as download: return new Response(buffer, { headers: { "Content-Type": "application/pdf", "Content-Disposition": "attachment; filename=invoice.pdf" } })
For complex PDFs: use puppeteer/playwright to render HTML to PDF (heavier but more flexible)
Note: @react-pdf works in serverless; puppeteer needs larger function size (use Inngest for background)

root_cause: Well-designed RLS policies provide security without sacrificing query performance
fix: Start restrictive: deny all, then add specific allow policies
Common patterns: owner-based (user_id = auth.uid()), role-based (auth.jwt() ->> role = admin), tenant-based
Performance: RLS adds WHERE clause to every query - ensure indexed columns are used in policies
Service role bypasses RLS: use only on server for admin operations, never expose key to client
Test policies: use Supabase SQL editor with SET ROLE authenticated; SET request.jwt.claims...
Avoid complex policies: joins in policies are expensive; denormalize if needed for performance

symptoms: implement scroll to top on navigation
root_cause: Reset scroll position when navigating between pages in Next.js App Router
fix: Next.js App Router handles scroll restoration automatically for most cases
Force scroll to top: router.push(url, { scroll: true }) // default behavior
Disable auto-scroll: <Link href={url} scroll={false}>Link</Link>
Smooth scroll to top: window.scrollTo({ top: 0, behavior: "smooth" })
For hash links: <Link href="#section"> scrolls to element with that ID
Save scroll position for back/forward: Next.js handles this automatically with App Router
Custom scroll restoration: use usePathname() + useEffect to control scroll on route change

root_cause: Isolated test environments prevent test data from corrupting development or production
fix: Unit tests: mock database entirely (vitest-mock-extended for Prisma)
Integration tests: use separate test database (SQLite in-memory or dedicated Neon branch)
E2E tests: run against preview deployment with seeded test data
Never share database between dev and test: tests may delete/modify data unexpectedly
Reset between test suites: prisma migrate reset or truncate all tables
Environment: TEST_DATABASE_URL separate from DATABASE_URL
CI: create ephemeral database per test run, destroy after (Neon branching is perfect for this)

root_cause: Large data exports should run as background jobs with progress tracking
fix: 1. User clicks Export → server action creates job: await inngest.send({ name: "export/start", data: { userId, filters, format: "csv" } })
2. Return job ID immediately: return { jobId } → show progress UI
3. Inngest function: step.run("query-data") → step.run("generate-file") → step.run("upload-to-blob")
4. Track progress in Redis: await redis.hset("export:"+jobId, { status: "processing", progress: 50 })
5. Client polls progress: useEffect with interval checking /api/export-status?jobId=xxx
6. On complete: store download URL in Redis, show download button to user
7. Cleanup: delete file and status after 24 hours via scheduled Inngest function

root_cause: First-time Vercel deployment checklist for a Next.js application
fix: 1. Connect GitHub repo in Vercel dashboard (auto-deploys on push to main)
2. Set all environment variables for Production AND Preview environments
3. Verify build succeeds: check Vercel deployment logs for errors
4. Configure custom domain: Vercel Dashboard > Domains > Add domain
5. Set up database: ensure Neon/Supabase/Turso is in same region as Vercel functions
6. Run migrations against production database: npx prisma migrate deploy
7. Verify: test all critical flows on production URL before announcing
8. Enable: Vercel Analytics, Speed Insights, and external error tracking (Sentry)

root_cause: Cookie consent implementation for GDPR/privacy compliance
fix: Check consent: const consent = cookies().get("cookie-consent")?.value
Banner component: show if no consent cookie set, with Accept/Decline buttons
On accept: set cookie with 1-year expiry: cookies().set("cookie-consent", "accepted", { maxAge: 365*24*60*60 })
Conditional loading: only load analytics/tracking scripts after consent: if (consent === "accepted") { loadAnalytics() }
Use next/script with conditional rendering: {hasConsent && <Script src="analytics.js" />}
Categories: allow granular consent (necessary, analytics, marketing) with checkboxes
Store preference: respect "declined" - don't keep asking (re-show only from privacy settings)

root_cause: Drizzle ORM provides type-safe SQL with minimal overhead for both SQLite and PostgreSQL
fix: Install for Turso: npm install drizzle-orm @libsql/client && npm i -D drizzle-kit
Install for Neon: npm install drizzle-orm @neondatabase/serverless && npm i -D drizzle-kit
Define schema: export const posts = pgTable("posts", { id: serial("id").primaryKey(), title: text("title").notNull(), createdAt: timestamp("created_at").defaultNow() })
Query: const allPosts = await db.select().from(posts).where(eq(posts.userId, id)).orderBy(desc(posts.createdAt))
Insert: await db.insert(posts).values({ title: "Hello" }).returning()
Type inference: type Post = typeof posts.$inferSelect; type NewPost = typeof posts.$inferInsert
Benefits vs Prisma: lighter bundle, closer to SQL, better for edge runtime, faster cold starts

root_cause: Proper cache headers reduce server load and improve response times for repeat visitors
fix: Static assets: Vercel auto-sets immutable cache headers for _next/static/ files
API responses: set Cache-Control based on data freshness: public, s-maxage=60, stale-while-revalidate=300
Private data: Cache-Control: private, no-store (never cache user-specific data on CDN)
ISR pages: Vercel manages caching automatically based on revalidate config
Set in route handler: return new Response(data, { headers: { "Cache-Control": "public, s-maxage=3600" } })
Vary header: add Vary: Accept-Encoding, Cookie if response differs by these
Purge cache: redeploy (Vercel purges on deploy) or use on-demand revalidation

root_cause: Multi-layer spam prevention for public forms without CAPTCHAs
fix: Layer 1 - Honeypot: add hidden field, reject if filled: <input name="website" className="hidden" />; if (formData.get("website")) return { error: "spam" }
Layer 2 - Time check: record form load time, reject if submitted < 2 seconds (bots are fast)
Layer 3 - Rate limit: limit to 3 submissions per IP per hour with Upstash Ratelimit
Layer 4 - Validation: strict Zod schema rejects malformed data
Server action: check all layers before processing: if (honeypot || tooFast || rateLimited) return quietly (don't reveal which check failed)
Optional Layer 5: Turnstile (Cloudflare) or hCaptcha for high-value forms

symptoms: headers() was called outside a request scope
Route used cookies()/headers() but they returned a Promise
root_cause: Next.js 15 made cookies(), headers(), draftMode() and route params/searchParams asynchronous; calling them synchronously throws or returns a Promise
fix: Await the call: const cookieStore = await cookies()
Await params in pages: export default async function Page({ params }) { const { slug } = await params }
Await searchParams the same way: const { q } = await searchParams
Run the codemod: npx @next/codemod@canary next-async-request-api .
Update types: params is now Promise<{ slug: string }>

root_cause: Synchronous access to dynamic params/searchParams is deprecated in Next.js 15 and will break in a future major
fix: Mark the component async and await params: const { id } = await params
In client components use the `use()` hook: const { id } = use(params)
For generateMetadata: async function generateMetadata({ params }) { const p = await params }
Run: npx @next/codemod@canary next-async-request-api .

symptoms: fetch no longer cached by default after upgrade
data refetches on every request in Next 15
root_cause: Next.js 15 changed defaults: fetch requests and GET Route Handlers are no longer cached by default (previously force-cache)
fix: Opt back into caching per fetch: fetch(url, { cache: 'force-cache' })
For GET route handlers add: export const dynamic = 'force-static'
Set default caching for a segment: export const fetchCache = 'default-cache'
Use time-based revalidation: fetch(url, { next: { revalidate: 3600 } })
Prefer explicit cache directives over relying on defaults

root_cause: Partial Prerendering serves a static shell instantly while streaming dynamic parts wrapped in Suspense
fix: Enable in next.config.js: experimental: { ppr: 'incremental' }
Opt a route in: export const experimental_ppr = true
Wrap dynamic content (using cookies/headers/uncached fetch) in <Suspense fallback={...}>
Everything outside Suspense becomes the prerendered static shell
PPR is experimental and requires Next.js canary; test thoroughly before production

root_cause: React 19's useActionState manages server action state and pending status, replacing the deprecated useFormState
fix: const [state, formAction, isPending] = useActionState(serverAction, initialState)
Pass formAction to the <form action={formAction}> attribute
Server action signature: async function action(prevState, formData) { ... return newState }

Show validation errors from state: {state?.error &&

{state.error}

}
useFormState from react-dom is deprecated; import useActionState from 'react'

root_cause: React 19's useOptimistic shows an immediate optimistic state that reverts automatically if the action fails
fix: const [optimistic, addOptimistic] = useOptimistic(items, (state, newItem) => [...state, newItem])
Call addOptimistic(newItem) before awaiting the server action
Render from optimistic state so the UI updates instantly
On error or revalidation React reconciles back to the real server state
Combine with useActionState for pending + optimistic together

root_cause: useFormStatus reads the parent form's submission status without prop drilling, ideal for submit buttons
fix: Create a SubmitButton client component: const { pending } = useFormStatus()
Return <button disabled={pending}>{pending ? 'Saving...' : 'Save'}</button>
The component MUST be a child of the <form>, not the form itself
Import from 'react-dom': import { useFormStatus } from 'react-dom'
Works automatically with Server Action forms for progressive enhancement

root_cause: Auth.js (NextAuth v5) restructures config into a single auth.ts exporting handlers, auth, signIn, signOut
fix: Install: npm install next-auth@beta
Create auth.ts: export const { handlers, auth, signIn, signOut } = NextAuth({ providers: [GitHub] })
Route handler app/api/auth/[...nextauth]/route.ts: export const { GET, POST } = handlers
Read session anywhere on the server: const session = await auth()
Set AUTH_SECRET env var (replaces NEXTAUTH_SECRET); generate with npx auth secret
Protect routes in middleware.ts: export { auth as middleware } from './auth'

root_cause: Clerk provides drop-in auth components and middleware-based route protection for Next.js
fix: Install: npm install @clerk/nextjs
Wrap root layout in <ClerkProvider>
Add middleware.ts: export default clerkMiddleware() with matcher config
Protect routes: const { userId } = await auth(); if (!userId) redirect('/sign-in')
Use prebuilt components: <SignInButton>, <UserButton>, <SignedIn>, <SignedOut>
Set NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY in Vercel env

symptoms: how to use Vercel AI SDK
root_cause: The Vercel AI SDK provides streamText/generateText and useChat for building streaming AI apps
fix: Install: npm install ai @ai-sdk/openai (or @ai-sdk/anthropic)
Route handler: const result = streamText({ model: openai('gpt-4o'), messages }); return result.toDataStreamResponse()
Client: const { messages, input, handleSubmit } = useChat()
Use streamObject for structured/typed streaming output with a Zod schema
Set provider keys (OPENAI_API_KEY / ANTHROPIC_API_KEY) in Vercel env vars
Set route maxDuration high enough for long completions: export const maxDuration = 60

root_cause: AI SDK tools let the model call typed functions; results are fed back for multi-step reasoning
fix: Define tools with Zod parameters: tools: { getWeather: tool({ parameters: z.object({ city: z.string() }), execute: async ({ city }) => fetchWeather(city) }) }
Enable multi-step: streamText({ ..., maxSteps: 5 })
The model decides when to call tools; execute runs server-side
Stream tool calls to the UI via toDataStreamResponse and render with useChat's message.parts
Validate all tool inputs since they come from the model

symptoms: how to use Vercel Edge Config
root_cause: Edge Config stores small JSON data read with near-zero latency from middleware and edge functions
fix: Install: npm install @vercel/edge-config
Create an Edge Config in the Vercel dashboard and link it to the project
Read: import { get } from '@vercel/edge-config'; const flag = await get('maintenanceMode')
Ideal for feature flags, A/B config, redirects, and allow/deny lists read in middleware
Writes go through the Vercel API or dashboard, not the read SDK; it is read-optimized
EDGE_CONFIG connection string is set automatically when linked

root_cause: Toggle a maintenance page instantly using Edge Config read in middleware, no redeploy needed
fix: Store { maintenance: true } in Edge Config
In middleware.ts: const isMaintenance = await get('maintenance')
If true and path !== '/maintenance': return NextResponse.rewrite(new URL('/maintenance', request.url))
Allow an admin bypass via a cookie or query param check
Flip the flag in the Vercel dashboard to enable/disable in seconds

root_cause: Next.js generates OG images at the edge using ImageResponse from next/og with JSX
fix: Create app/blog/[slug]/opengraph-image.tsx
export const size = { width: 1200, height: 630 }; export const contentType = 'image/png'

Default export: return new ImageResponse(
{title}
, { ...size })
Use inline styles only (flexbox); className/Tailwind is NOT supported in ImageResponse
Load custom fonts by passing fonts: [{ name, data, weight }] in options
Fetch dynamic data (title) inside the function from params

root_cause: Next.js generates robots.txt from a typed app/robots.ts file
fix: Create app/robots.ts exporting a default function returning MetadataRoute.Robots
return { rules: { userAgent: '*', allow: '/', disallow: '/admin/' }, sitemap: 'https://site.com/sitemap.xml' }
Reference an absolute sitemap URL using your production domain
Block preview deployments from indexing by checking process.env.VERCEL_ENV !== 'production'
File is served at /robots.txt automatically

root_cause: A typed app/manifest.ts produces manifest.webmanifest for installable PWAs
fix: Create app/manifest.ts returning MetadataRoute.Manifest
Include name, short_name, start_url: '/', display: 'standalone', icons with sizes 192 and 512
Set theme_color and background_color to match your brand
Next.js links it automatically; no manual <link rel='manifest'> needed
For full offline support pair with a service worker (e.g. Serwist)

symptoms: open content in modal but full page on refresh
root_cause: Intercepting routes show content in a modal when navigated client-side but render the full page on hard refresh
fix: Create the modal slot with parallel route: app/@modal/(.)photos/[id]/page.tsx
The (.) marker intercepts same-level segments; (..) intercepts one level up
Add @modal slot to layout: function Layout({ children, modal }) { return <>{children}{modal}</> }
Provide a default.tsx returning null so the slot renders nothing when inactive
On hard navigation to /photos/[id] the real page renders instead of the modal

root_cause: Draft Mode bypasses static caching to render draft CMS content for editors
fix: Enable route: app/api/draft/route.ts -> const draft = await draftMode(); draft.enable(); redirect(slug)
Validate a secret token and the slug before enabling to prevent abuse
In pages: const { isEnabled } = await draftMode(); fetch published or draft accordingly
Pages with draft mode on are rendered dynamically (no static cache)
Disable route calls draftMode().disable()

symptoms: dynamic page title and meta tags
root_cause: generateMetadata fetches data to build per-route metadata, deduplicated with the page's own fetches
fix: export async function generateMetadata({ params }) { const post = await getPost((await params).slug) }
Return { title: post.title, description: post.excerpt, openGraph: { images: [post.image] } }
fetch calls are deduped with the page via React cache, so no double request
Use a title.template in the root layout for site-wide suffixes
Return metadataBase in root layout so relative OG image URLs resolve

symptoms: show instant UI while data loads
root_cause: Wrapping slow async server components in Suspense streams a fallback first, then the content
fix: Keep fast content outside Suspense so it renders immediately
Move the slow fetch into a child async component and wrap it: <Suspense fallback={<Skeleton />}><SlowList /></Suspense>
Each Suspense boundary streams independently as its data resolves
Do NOT await the slow fetch in the parent or you block the whole page
Use loading.tsx for route-level streaming of the entire page

symptoms: self is not defined during build
root_cause: Browser-only globals are accessed during server-side rendering or build where they don't exist
fix: Guard access: if (typeof window !== 'undefined') { ... }
Move browser-only logic into useEffect (runs only on the client)
Dynamically import the offending component with ssr disabled: dynamic(() => import('./C'), { ssr: false })
For libraries using window at import time, import them inside useEffect or a client component
'self is not defined' usually means a client-only lib is bundled into a Server Component

symptoms: NEXT_REDIRECT error caught in catch block
root_cause: redirect() and notFound() work by throwing a special error; a surrounding try/catch swallows it
fix: Call redirect() OUTSIDE try/catch blocks
If you must, re-throw it: catch (e) { if (isRedirectError(e)) throw e; ... }
Import the guard: import { isRedirectError } from 'next/dist/client/components/redirect'
Same applies to notFound() which throws NEXT_NOT_FOUND
Place redirect after the try/catch once the success path is known

symptoms: No Next.js version detected on Vercel
root_cause: Vercel cannot locate the Next.js app, usually due to a wrong Root Directory in a monorepo
fix: Set Root Directory in Vercel Project Settings to the app folder (e.g. apps/web)
Ensure next is in dependencies (not only devDependencies) of that package's package.json
For monorepos, set the correct Install/Build commands or use the framework preset
Confirm package.json has a build script: "build": "next build"
Check that the lockfile at repo root includes the app's dependencies

symptoms: Prisma works locally fails on deploy
root_cause: Vercel caches dependencies so prisma generate doesn't run on rebuilds, leaving a stale client
fix: Add a postinstall script: "postinstall": "prisma generate"
Or prepend to build: "build": "prisma generate && next build"
For Prisma 5+ set generator output explicitly to avoid node_modules caching issues
Ensure DATABASE_URL is set in Vercel env for the build if needed
Clear the Vercel build cache and redeploy if it persists

root_cause: Vercel Cron requires a crons entry in vercel.json pointing to a GET route handler
fix: Add to vercel.json: { "crons": [{ "path": "/api/cron/cleanup", "schedule": "0 2 * * *" }] }
Create the route handler app/api/cron/cleanup/route.ts exporting GET
Secure it: verify Authorization header equals `Bearer ${process.env.CRON_SECRET}`
Crons run only on Production deployments, not Preview
Hobby plan allows limited cron frequency; check plan limits
Schedule uses UTC cron syntax

symptoms: skip Vercel build when no changes
avoid rebuilding unchanged app in monorepo
root_cause: Use Vercel's Ignored Build Step to skip deploys when a project's files didn't change
fix: Set Ignored Build Step command in Project Settings
For Turborepo: npx turbo-ignore
Custom git check: git diff HEAD^ HEAD --quiet -- ./apps/web && exit 0 || exit 1
Exit code 0 = skip build, exit code 1 = build
Reduces build minutes and noise in monorepos with many apps

symptoms: large file upload to route fails
root_cause: Vercel functions and Server Actions cap request body size (Server Actions default ~1MB)
fix: Increase Server Action limit: next.config.js -> experimental: { serverActions: { bodySizeLimit: '5mb' } }
For large files, upload directly to storage with a presigned URL (Vercel Blob client upload, S3)
Don't proxy big files through the function; stream client -> storage directly
Vercel function request body is limited (4.5MB on serverless); use client uploads above that
Validate and reject oversized uploads early on the client

root_cause: output: 'standalone' produces a minimal self-contained server for Docker/self-hosting
fix: next.config.js: output: 'standalone'
Build copies a minimal node_modules into .next/standalone
Run with: node .next/standalone/server.js
Copy .next/static and public into the standalone folder in your Dockerfile
Set HOSTNAME=0.0.0.0 and PORT in the container
Use a multi-stage Dockerfile to keep the final image small

root_cause: Validate env vars with a schema so misconfiguration fails at build/boot instead of at runtime
fix: Use @t3-oss/env-nextjs with Zod to define server and client schemas
Separate server vars from client (NEXT_PUBLIC_) so secrets never reach the bundle
Import the validated env object everywhere instead of process.env
Validation runs at build time and throws on missing/invalid vars
Add a runtime skip flag (SKIP_ENV_VALIDATION) for Docker build steps if needed

root_cause: Third-party scripts loaded in <head> block the main thread; next/script controls load timing
fix: Use next/script with strategy: 'afterInteractive' for analytics (default)
Use strategy: 'lazyOnload' for chat widgets and non-critical scripts
Use strategy: 'worker' (Partytown, experimental) to offload to a web worker
Never inline heavy third-party JS in the document head
Audit impact in Lighthouse's 'Reduce the impact of third-party code'

root_cause: Marking large trees 'use client' ships and hydrates unnecessary JS, hurting TBT/INP
fix: Keep components as Server Components by default; push 'use client' to the leaves
Pass server-fetched data down as props instead of fetching in client components
Wrap only the interactive island in a client component, not its container
Pass Server Components as children to client components to keep them server-rendered
Measure with the bundle analyzer and the Network tab's JS payload

symptoms: CLS caused by custom font swap
root_cause: Custom fonts cause CLS when the fallback font has different metrics than the web font
fix: Use next/font which auto-generates a size-adjusted fallback to eliminate shift
import { Inter } from 'next/font/google'; const inter = Inter({ subsets: ['latin'], display: 'swap' })
Apply via className on <html> so it's available app-wide
For local fonts use next/font/local with explicit fallback metrics
Self-hosting via next/font removes the extra request to Google Fonts

root_cause: Vercel injects geo data into requests; middleware can route or localize based on it
fix: In middleware: const country = request.headers.get('x-vercel-ip-country')
On older API use request.geo?.country (deprecated in newer Next)
Redirect or rewrite based on region: NextResponse.rewrite(new URL(`/${locale}${path}`, request.url))
Geo headers are only populated on Vercel, not local dev (mock them locally)
Cache the locale in a cookie to avoid re-evaluating every request

root_cause: Assign users to A/B buckets in middleware via a cookie and rewrite to the variant
fix: In middleware read or assign a bucket cookie: const bucket = cookie ?? (hash % 2 ? 'a' : 'b')
Rewrite to the variant path: NextResponse.rewrite(new URL(`/${bucket}${path}`, url))
Persist the bucket: response.cookies.set('bucket', bucket, { maxAge })
Read assignment config from Edge Config for instant rollout changes
Avoid Math.random per request for sticky assignment; hash a stable user id

root_cause: Implement HTTP Basic Auth in middleware to protect staging or admin areas
fix: In middleware check the Authorization header for 'Basic ' + base64(user:pass)
Compare against env vars BASIC_AUTH_USER / BASIC_AUTH_PASSWORD
If missing/invalid: return new NextResponse('Auth required', { status: 401, headers: { 'WWW-Authenticate': 'Basic realm="protected"' } })
Scope it with the matcher to only protect certain paths
Use timing-safe comparison and HTTPS only; Basic Auth is weak alone

root_cause: After a mutation in a Server Action, revalidate cached data so the UI reflects changes
fix: After a write call revalidatePath('/posts') to purge that route's cache
Tag fetches: fetch(url, { next: { tags: ['posts'] } }) then revalidateTag('posts')
revalidateTag is more granular and updates every fetch sharing the tag
Call revalidate inside the Server Action after the DB write succeeds
For client-triggered refresh use router.refresh() to re-fetch server components

symptoms: UI shows stale content after mutation
have to hard refresh to see changes
root_cause: The Server Action mutated data but never revalidated the cache, so the cached render is served
fix: Add revalidatePath or revalidateTag at the end of the action after the write
If using a client component, call router.refresh() after the action resolves
Check the fetch wasn't force-cached without a revalidate tag
Confirm the action actually awaited the DB write before revalidating
For optimistic UI, pair useOptimistic with revalidation as the source of truth

root_cause: React Strict Mode intentionally double-invokes effects in development to surface side-effect bugs
fix: This only happens in development, not production builds
Don't disable Strict Mode to hide it; fix non-idempotent effects instead
Move data fetching to Server Components or React Query/SWR which dedupe
Add cleanup or an AbortController to cancel the duplicate request
Verify by running next build && next start where it runs once

symptoms: env var works in build but undefined at runtime
env value baked at build time
root_cause: NEXT_PUBLIC_ vars are inlined at build time; changing them in Vercel requires a redeploy
fix: NEXT_PUBLIC_ values are frozen into the client bundle at build, not read at runtime
After changing a NEXT_PUBLIC_ var in Vercel, redeploy to pick up the new value
Server-only vars ARE read at runtime and can change without rebuild
Don't expect runtime mutation of public env vars
Use Edge Config or a server fetch for values that must change without redeploy

root_cause: Awaiting requests one-by-one in a loop creates a waterfall; independent fetches should run in parallel
fix: Replace for-loop awaits with Promise.all over the mapped promises
const results = await Promise.all(ids.map(id => getItem(id)))
Initiate independent fetches before awaiting to overlap them
Only sequence when a later request genuinely depends on an earlier result
Watch for hidden N+1 in render: fetch a batch, not per-item

symptoms: use client at the top of layout
whole app is a client component
root_cause: Putting 'use client' high in the tree opts every descendant into client rendering, losing RSC benefits
fix: Keep layouts and pages as Server Components
Extract only the interactive piece (e.g. a theme toggle, menu) into a small client component
Wrap a client Provider around {children} but keep children server-rendered by passing them as a prop
A provider needing 'use client' does not force its children to be client components
Audit which files have 'use client' and push them toward the leaves

symptoms: works in dev but breaks in production build
feature only fails after next build
root_cause: Dev and production differ in caching, minification, env, and Strict Mode timing; reproduce locally first
fix: Reproduce with next build && next start locally instead of next dev
Check for code relying on dev-only double render or unminified behavior
Verify all required env vars exist in the Vercel environment (not just .env.local)
Look for caching assumptions: prod caches aggressively, dev doesn't
Inspect Vercel build logs and runtime logs for the failing route
Confirm dependencies needed at runtime aren't only in devDependencies

symptoms: slow navigation due to large flight data
root_cause: Large server-to-client RSC payloads slow navigation; trim what crosses the boundary
fix: Don't pass huge serialized objects as props from Server to Client Components
Select only the fields the client needs before passing data down
Avoid serializing entire ORM rows; map to a minimal DTO
Inspect the .rsc/flight payload in the Network tab during navigation
Keep heavy read-only data in Server Components rather than hydrating it

root_cause: Stripe Billing Portal lets customers manage subscriptions, payment methods, and invoices
fix: Store the Stripe customerId on your user record at signup/first purchase
Create a server action/route: stripe.billingPortal.sessions.create({ customer, return_url })
Redirect the user to session.url
Configure portal features (cancel, update plan) in the Stripe Dashboard
Handle customer.subscription.updated/deleted webhooks to sync entitlement
Gate access by checking subscription status from your DB, not Stripe, on each request

symptoms: send transactional email with React Email
root_cause: React Email builds responsive email templates as React components, rendered and sent via Resend
fix: Install: npm install react-email @react-email/components resend
Create templates in /emails as React components using @react-email/components
Preview locally with: email dev
Render and send: await resend.emails.send({ from, to, subject, react: <WelcomeEmail name={name} /> })
Verify your sending domain in Resend and set RESEND_API_KEY
Send from a Server Action or background job, not the client

symptoms: large file upload to Vercel Blob
upload big files without function limit
root_cause: Use Vercel Blob client uploads to send large files directly from the browser, bypassing function body limits
fix: Install: npm install @vercel/blob
Create a route handler that returns upload tokens via handleUpload from @vercel/blob/client
Client: upload(file.name, file, { access: 'public', handleUploadUrl: '/api/upload' })
The file goes browser -> Blob storage directly, not through your function
Validate allowed content types and size in onBeforeGenerateToken
Persist the returned blob.url to your database in onUploadCompleted

root_cause: Magic link auth emails a one-time signed link that logs the user in without a password
fix: With Auth.js use the Email/Resend provider; it stores a verification token
Configure an adapter (Prisma/Drizzle) to persist users and tokens
User submits email -> token row created -> email sent with callback URL
Clicking the link verifies the token and creates a session
Set token expiry short (e.g. 10 minutes) and single-use
Rate-limit the request endpoint to prevent email bombing

symptoms: should I use route handlers or separate API
root_cause: Route Handlers act as a Backend-for-Frontend, keeping secrets server-side and shaping data for the UI
fix: Use Route Handlers / Server Actions as the BFF layer for your own frontend
Server Components can call your data layer directly without an HTTP hop
Expose Route Handlers when external/mobile clients or webhooks need HTTP endpoints
Keep third-party API keys and DB access server-side; never proxy secrets to the client
Version public API routes (/api/v1) but internal Server Actions can evolve freely
Consider a separate service only when scaling/ownership diverges from the web app

symptoms: where to cache in Next.js stack
root_cause: Layer caches by scope: CDN/full-route for static, ISR for revalidatable pages, Redis for shared dynamic data
fix: Edge/CDN cache: static assets and fully static routes, served globally
Full Route Cache + ISR: pages that change occasionally (revalidate or on-demand)
Data Cache: fetch responses, tagged for granular invalidation
Request memoization (React cache): dedupe within a single render
Redis/KV: cross-request shared state, sessions, rate limits, expensive computed results
Invalidate from the layer that owns the data after each mutation

root_cause: A consistent error envelope makes clients handle failures uniformly across all routes
fix: Return a stable shape: { error: { code: 'NOT_FOUND', message: 'Post not found' } }
Use machine-readable codes (UPPER_SNAKE) plus a human message
Map errors to correct HTTP status (400 validation, 401 auth, 403 forbidden, 404, 409 conflict, 500)
Never leak stack traces or internal details in production responses
For validation, include a field-level errors map from Zod's flatten()
Centralize with a helper: apiError(code, message, status)

root_cause: Essential SEO items to verify before launching a Next.js site
fix: 1. metadataBase set in root layout for absolute URLs
2. Unique generateMetadata title/description per route
3. opengraph-image and twitter card per key page
4. app/sitemap.ts and app/robots.ts generated and reachable
5. Canonical URLs set via alternates.canonical to avoid duplicates
6. Structured data (JSON-LD) for articles/products where relevant
7. Preview/staging deployments blocked from indexing (noindex)
8. Semantic HTML, alt text on images, fast LCP for ranking

root_cause: Core accessibility checks to run before shipping
fix: 1. All interactive elements reachable and operable by keyboard
2. Visible focus indicators (don't remove outline without a replacement)
3. Sufficient color contrast (4.5:1 for body text)
4. Images have meaningful alt text; decorative images alt=''
5. Form inputs have associated <label>s and error messaging
6. Use semantic landmarks (header/nav/main/footer) and heading order
7. ARIA only when native HTML can't express it; test with a screen reader
8. Respect prefers-reduced-motion for animations

root_cause: Observability essentials so you can detect and debug production issues
fix: 1. Error tracking (Sentry) capturing both server and client errors
2. Source maps uploaded so stack traces are readable
3. Vercel Analytics + Speed Insights for real-user Core Web Vitals
4. Structured server logs with request id correlation
5. Alerts on error rate, p95 latency, and function timeouts
6. Uptime/health-check monitoring on critical endpoints
7. Log retention and PII redaction policy in place

symptoms: Tailwind @apply unknown utility in CSS module
Tailwind directive fails in component css
root_cause: Tailwind directives in CSS modules need Tailwind processing and access to the config
fix: Ensure PostCSS/Tailwind processes the file (it does by default for imported CSS)
Only @apply utilities that exist in your config; custom ones must be defined
In Tailwind v4 prefer the @reference directive at the top of CSS modules to import theme
Avoid @apply for one-offs; use utility classes directly in JSX
Restart the dev server after changing tailwind.config or globals

root_cause: Without a blocking script the server renders a default theme, causing a flash before client hydration
fix: Wrap the app in next-themes ThemeProvider with attribute='class'
Add suppressHydrationWarning to the <html> element in root layout
Set defaultTheme='system' and enableSystem for OS preference
next-themes injects a pre-hydration script that sets the class before paint
Avoid reading theme during SSR; render theme-dependent UI after mount

symptoms: image with fill has zero height
root_cause: An image with the fill prop needs a positioned, sized parent to fill
fix: Set the parent to position: relative (or absolute/fixed) with explicit dimensions
Provide the sizes prop so the right source is chosen: sizes='(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw'
Use object-fit via className: className='object-cover'
Without a sized parent the image has no box to fill and collapses
For known dimensions prefer width/height over fill

root_cause: Next.js 14+ moved viewport and themeColor out of the metadata export into a separate viewport export
fix: Replace metadata.viewport/themeColor with: export const viewport = { themeColor: '#fff', width: 'device-width' }
Type it as Viewport from 'next'
Keep other fields in the metadata export
generateViewport() is the dynamic equivalent if values depend on data
Run the build to confirm the warning is gone

root_cause: next/dynamic expects a module with a default export; named exports need a .then mapping
fix: For default export: const C = dynamic(() => import('./C'))
For named export: const C = dynamic(() => import('./C').then(m => m.Named))
Disable SSR for browser-only components: { ssr: false } (only in Client Components in Next 15)
Provide a loading fallback: { loading: () => <Skeleton /> }
ssr:false is not allowed in Server Components; move it to a client boundary

symptoms: Error: Invariant: headers() expects to have requestAsyncStorage
cookies/headers used in a module top-level
root_cause: Request-scoped APIs (cookies/headers/auth) were called outside a request, e.g. at module load or in a non-request context
fix: Only call cookies()/headers() inside a component, Route Handler, or Server Action during a request
Don't call them at the top level of a module or in shared utilities imported at build time
For Server Actions ensure 'use server' and that the function runs per-request
Avoid calling them in generateStaticParams (no request exists then)
Refactor shared helpers to receive the value as an argument instead of reading it globally

symptoms: SSG vs SSR vs ISR vs PPR
when to use which rendering mode
root_cause: Pick rendering per route based on data freshness needs and personalization
fix: Static (SSG): content same for all users, changes rarely -> fastest, cache at CDN
ISR: mostly static but updates periodically -> revalidate by time or on-demand
Dynamic (SSR): per-request/personalized data -> uses cookies/headers, no static cache
PPR: static shell + dynamic islands in one route -> best of both (experimental)
Default to static; opt into dynamic only where you read request data
Mixing: keep the page static and stream the personalized part in Suspense

root_cause: generateStaticParams pre-renders dynamic routes at build; balance build time vs on-demand generation
fix: Return the params to prebuild: export async function generateStaticParams() { return posts.map(p => ({ slug: p.slug })) }
Prebuild only high-traffic/known pages; let the long tail render on-demand
Control unknown paths with dynamicParams (true = render on demand, false = 404)
Combine with revalidate for ISR so prebuilt pages refresh
Avoid prebuilding thousands of rarely-visited pages (slow builds)

root_cause: Turborepo structures a monorepo with shared packages and cached task pipelines
fix: Layout: apps/ (web, docs) and packages/ (ui, config, db)
Share internal packages with workspace:* and transpilePackages in next.config
Define a turbo.json pipeline with build/lint/test and proper dependsOn
Use remote caching on Vercel to share build artifacts across CI and team
Keep shared tsconfig/eslint in packages/config
Use turbo-ignore for Vercel Ignored Build Step to skip unaffected apps

root_cause: Virtualize long lists so only visible rows are in the DOM, keeping memory and render cost low
fix: Use @tanstack/react-virtual or react-window in a client component
Only render items in (and slightly around) the viewport
Combine with server-side pagination or infinite scroll to limit data fetched
Give rows stable keys and fixed/estimated heights for smooth scrolling
Avoid heavy per-row components; memoize row rendering
Don't virtualize short lists; it adds complexity for no gain

root_cause: Rotate secrets regularly and support overlapping keys to rotate without downtime
fix: Store secrets only in Vercel env vars / a secrets manager, never in the repo
Support two valid keys during rotation (accept old + new) then retire the old
Rotate immediately if a secret is ever exposed (assume compromised)
Scope keys narrowly (least privilege) so a leak's blast radius is small
Audit access and set calendar reminders for periodic rotation
Redeploy after rotating NEXT_PUBLIC values since they're build-baked

root_cause: Server Action forms submit via native HTML when JS hasn't loaded, then enhance once hydrated
fix: Use <form action={serverAction}> with the action receiving FormData
Read values server-side: const name = formData.get('name')
Avoid relying on onClick/onChange for core submission logic
Use useActionState for validation feedback that also degrades gracefully
Test by disabling JS: the form should still submit and redirect
Add a hidden input for ids instead of closures when you need extra data

root_cause: useTransition marks updates as non-urgent so the UI stays responsive and can show pending state
fix: const [isPending, startTransition] = useTransition()
Wrap the action: startTransition(() => { router.push(url) }) or call a server action
Use isPending to show a spinner without blocking input
Great for filtering/searching large lists and router navigations
Server Actions invoked in a transition give you pending state for free

root_cause: Use the async cookies() API to read and write cookies in Server Actions and Route Handlers
fix: const cookieStore = await cookies() (await required in Next 15)
Read: cookieStore.get('session')?.value
Set (only in Server Action or Route Handler): cookieStore.set('session', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'lax', path: '/' })
Delete: cookieStore.delete('session')
You cannot set cookies during a normal Server Component render (read-only there)
Use httpOnly + secure for auth tokens

root_cause: Catch-all segments match nested paths; optional catch-all also matches the base path
fix: Catch-all: app/docs/[...slug]/page.tsx matches /docs/a/b (params.slug = ['a','b'])
Optional catch-all: app/shop/[[...slug]]/page.tsx ALSO matches /shop (slug undefined)
Await params then handle the array: const { slug } = await params
Pair with generateStaticParams returning { slug: ['a','b'] } arrays
Use for docs, nested categories, or CMS-driven paths

symptoms: share state across server and client with context
hydrate server data into client store
root_cause: Initialize client state from server-fetched data by passing it through a client provider once
fix: Fetch data in a Server Component, pass it as props to a Client provider
In the provider, initialize the store with the server data on first render only
For Zustand, create the store per-request with a context provider to avoid cross-request leakage
Don't put a module-level store with server data (it leaks between users on the server)
Re-sync on navigation with router.refresh() or revalidation rather than client refetch

root_cause: A health endpoint reports app and dependency status for uptime monitors and load balancers
fix: Create app/api/health/route.ts exporting GET
export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' so it isn't cached
Check critical deps (DB ping, Redis ping) with a short timeout
Return 200 { status: 'ok' } or 503 { status: 'degraded', checks } on failure
Keep it lightweight; don't run expensive queries on every probe
Point Vercel/uptime monitors at this route

symptoms: generate sitemap for thousands of URLs
root_cause: For large sites, generate multiple sitemaps with generateSitemaps to stay under the 50k URL limit
fix: Export generateSitemaps returning [{ id: 0 }, { id: 1 }, ...] chunks
In sitemap({ id }) return the slice of URLs for that id (each <= 50,000)
Next.js serves them at /sitemap/0.xml, /sitemap/1.xml plus an index
Fetch URL ranges from the DB per chunk to avoid loading everything
Reference the sitemap index in robots.ts

root_cause: Stream a file as a download response with proper headers instead of buffering it in memory
fix: Return a Response with the readable stream as the body
Set Content-Disposition: 'attachment; filename="report.csv"'
Set the correct Content-Type (e.g. text/csv, application/pdf)
For generated content, use a ReadableStream and enqueue chunks
For files already in storage, redirect to a signed URL instead of proxying
Set export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' to avoid caching the download

root_cause: Validate redirect targets to prevent open-redirect attacks via attacker-controlled URLs
fix: Only allow relative paths: reject values not starting with a single '/'
Reject '//' and 'http(s):' prefixes which can redirect off-site
Maintain an allowlist of permitted redirect destinations when possible
const safe = next?.startsWith('/') && !next.startsWith('//') ? next : '/dashboard'
Apply this to the callbackUrl / next param after auth flows

root_cause: Set a Content Security Policy and security headers via middleware or next.config headers()
fix: Generate a per-request nonce in middleware and add it to the CSP header
Pass the nonce to inline scripts: <script nonce={nonce}>; Next reads it for its own scripts
Set headers: X-Frame-Options DENY, X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy
Use Strict-Transport-Security for HTTPS enforcement
Start CSP in report-only mode to catch violations before enforcing
Avoid 'unsafe-inline' for scripts; use the nonce instead

root_cause: Reading cookies/headers or using no-store fetch opts the whole route into dynamic rendering, losing caching
fix: Check the build output: 'ƒ (Dynamic)' vs '○ (Static)' per route
Isolate dynamic reads (cookies/headers) into a Suspense-wrapped child so the rest stays static
Remove accidental cache: 'no-store' or export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic'
Use generateStaticParams + revalidate to keep dynamic-looking routes static
Consider PPR to keep a static shell with a dynamic island

symptoms: slow cold start from heavy imports
root_cause: Heavy top-level imports inflate the serverless bundle and slow cold starts
fix: Lazy-import heavy/rarely-used modules inside the handler instead of at top level
Avoid importing huge SDKs you only use on some code paths
Prefer smaller alternatives (e.g. date-fns over moment, jose over jsonwebtoken)
Keep edge functions lean; they have stricter size limits
Check bundle size per route in the build output and trim
Pin the function region near your database to cut total latency

root_cause: Vercel KV is a managed Redis (Upstash) integrated with Vercel for caching and simple data
fix: Create a KV store in the Vercel dashboard and connect it to the project
Install: npm install @vercel/kv
Use: import { kv } from '@vercel/kv'; await kv.set('key', value); await kv.get('key')
Connection env vars (KV_REST_API_URL/TOKEN) are injected automatically
Great for rate limiting, sessions, feature flags, and caching
It speaks the Upstash REST API so it works in edge and serverless

root_cause: drizzle-kit generates SQL migrations from your schema and applies them
fix: Configure drizzle.config.ts with schema path, dialect, and dbCredentials
Generate migrations from schema changes: npx drizzle-kit generate
Apply them: npx drizzle-kit migrate (or run the migrator at deploy)
Use drizzle-kit push for rapid prototyping (no migration files) in dev only
Commit generated SQL files; run migrate in CI/deploy, not at request time
For serverless, run migrations in a deploy step, not in the app runtime

root_cause: Next.js supports OpenTelemetry tracing via the instrumentation.ts hook
fix: Create instrumentation.ts at the project root exporting register()
Install @vercel/otel and call registerOTel({ serviceName }) inside register()
Next emits spans for requests, fetches, and rendering automatically
Export traces to your collector (e.g. via OTLP) by configuring the SDK
On Vercel, traces integrate with the observability/OTel drains
Add custom spans for important business operations

symptoms: how to setup TanStack Query with App Router
root_cause: TanStack Query integrates with the App Router by prefetching on the server and hydrating on the client
fix: Create a QueryClient and wrap client components in QueryClientProvider (a client component)
Prefetch in a Server Component: await queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey, queryFn })
Pass dehydrated state: <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(queryClient)}>
Make a fresh QueryClient per request (cache: () => makeClient) to avoid cross-user leaks
Use it for client-side caching, mutations, and background refetch on top of RSC data

symptoms: Server Action not found after deploy
stale action id after new deployment
root_cause: An open client tab references a Server Action id from an old deployment that no longer exists
fix: Tell users to refresh; the new bundle has new action ids
Handle gracefully: catch the error and prompt a reload
Use skew protection on Vercel to keep old deployments addressable during rollout
Avoid extremely long-lived tabs depending on actions across many deploys
Confirm the action file has 'use server' and is actually exported

root_cause: useSearchParams() in a component not wrapped in Suspense forces client-side rendering of the whole route
fix: Wrap the component using useSearchParams in <Suspense fallback={...}>
Keep it low in the tree so only that island bails out, not the page
This commonly appears at build with 'Entire page deopted into client-side rendering'
Alternatively read search params from the page's searchParams prop on the server
Provide a meaningful fallback to avoid layout shift

symptoms: ENOENT no such file in production
file read works locally fails on Vercel
root_cause: Reading files by relative path fails on Vercel because the runtime CWD and bundling differ from local
fix: Bundle data files via import instead of fs reads when possible
For files you must read, resolve from process.cwd() and include them via outputFileTracingIncludes
next.config.js: experimental.outputFileTracingIncludes to ship needed files into the function
Don't write to the filesystem at runtime (serverless FS is read-only except /tmp)
Use /tmp for transient writes; persist to Blob/S3 for durability

symptoms: pnpm lockfile out of date on Vercel
root_cause: Vercel installs with a frozen lockfile; an out-of-date pnpm-lock.yaml fails the build
fix: Run pnpm install locally and commit the updated pnpm-lock.yaml
Ensure the committed lockfile matches package.json exactly
Match the package manager version via packageManager field or corepack
Don't hand-edit package.json without regenerating the lockfile
For monorepos commit the single root lockfile

symptoms: catch errors in a route segment
root_cause: error.tsx catches errors in its segment; global-error.tsx catches errors in the root layout
fix: Create error.tsx as a Client Component with ({ error, reset }) props
Call reset() to retry rendering the segment
Errors in the root layout are only caught by app/global-error.tsx (must render <html>/<body>)
Log the error to Sentry inside a useEffect in error.tsx
not-found.tsx handles notFound() / 404s separately from errors
Server errors are sanitized in production; a digest links to server logs

root_cause: loading.tsx provides an instant loading UI while the segment's server component streams
fix: Create loading.tsx next to page.tsx; it wraps the page in a Suspense boundary automatically
Render a skeleton matching the final layout to minimize CLS
It shows immediately on navigation, before data resolves
Nest loading.tsx per segment for granular loading states
For finer control, use <Suspense> directly around slow sub-components

symptoms: prevent server code in client bundle
root_cause: The server-only/client-only packages throw at build if a module is imported into the wrong environment
fix: Install: npm install server-only client-only
Add import 'server-only' at the top of modules using secrets/DB so they can't reach the client
Add import 'client-only' to modules that must run only in the browser
Build fails fast with a clear error if the boundary is crossed
Use server-only for your data layer (db.ts, secret config)

symptoms: dynamic OG image with custom font
root_cause: Load a custom font into ImageResponse so OG images use your brand typeface
fix: Fetch the font as an ArrayBuffer at the top of the route (from public or a URL)
Pass it: new ImageResponse(jsx, { fonts: [{ name: 'Brand', data: fontData, weight: 700, style: 'normal' }] })
Reference it via fontFamily: 'Brand' in inline styles
Only include the weights/glyphs you use to keep it fast
Cache the font fetch with React cache to avoid refetching per image
Edge runtime can't read fs at runtime; fetch the font over HTTP or import it

root_cause: Verify inbound auth-provider webhooks (Clerk uses Svix) before trusting the payload
fix: Read the raw body (do not parse first): const body = await request.text()
Verify with the Svix library using the signing secret and svix-* headers
Reject with 400 if verification fails
Handle user.created/updated/deleted events to sync your DB
Store the webhook signing secret in env (e.g. CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET)
Make handlers idempotent; providers retry on non-2xx

root_cause: Drive pagination from the URL searchParams so pages are shareable and server-rendered
fix: Read the page from the page's searchParams prop: const { page = '1' } = await searchParams
Compute skip/take (or cursor) and fetch that slice on the server
Render prev/next as <Link href={`?page=${n}`}> so navigation is server-driven
Validate/clamp the page number to avoid invalid offsets
Prefer cursor pagination for large/changing datasets to avoid skip cost
Show total/last page only if cheap to compute

root_cause: Provide a not-found.tsx and call notFound() to render it for missing resources
fix: Create app/not-found.tsx for the root 404 (and per-segment for scoped 404s)
In a page, when a resource is missing: import { notFound } from 'next/navigation'; notFound()
notFound() throws NEXT_NOT_FOUND; call it outside try/catch
Customize copy and links back to safe pages
Set metadata in not-found via generateMetadata or a static export
Combine with generateStaticParams + dynamicParams=false to 404 unknown paths

root_cause: Use usePathname in a client component to style the active navigation link
fix: 'use client'; const pathname = usePathname()
const isActive = pathname === href || pathname.startsWith(href + '/')
Apply conditional classes: className={cn('nav', isActive && 'nav-active')}
Keep the nav a small client component; the surrounding layout stays server-rendered
next/link prefetches in the viewport by default for fast nav

root_cause: Update searchParams to reflect UI state (filters, tabs) so it's shareable and back-button friendly
fix: Build params: const params = new URLSearchParams(searchParams); params.set('sort', value)
Push or replace: router.replace(`${pathname}?${params.toString()}`)
Use replace for filter changes to avoid polluting history; push for meaningful navigation
Read the values server-side from searchParams to refetch filtered data
Consider the nuqs library for typed, ergonomic URL state
Wrap useSearchParams usage in Suspense to avoid client-render bailout

root_cause: Keep route handlers, Server Actions, and components thin; put domain logic in a reusable service/data layer
fix: Create src/lib/services or src/server with pure functions for business logic
Server Actions and Route Handlers validate input then delegate to services
Services own DB access (via Prisma/Drizzle) and are independently testable
Mark the data layer with import 'server-only'
Share Zod schemas between the boundary (validation) and the service signatures
This keeps logic reusable across actions, routes, and jobs

root_cause: Server Actions are public endpoints; always authenticate, authorize, and validate their inputs
fix: Re-check the session/user inside every action (don't rely on UI hiding the button)
Validate and coerce FormData with a Zod schema before using it
Authorize the specific resource (does this user own this record?)
Return typed errors via useActionState instead of throwing raw errors
Treat the action like any public API: rate limit sensitive ones
Never pass closures with secrets; they aren't a security boundary

symptoms: mutating data in a GET route
root_cause: GET requests must be safe and idempotent; mutations belong in POST/Server Actions
fix: Never write to the DB or trigger side effects in a GET route handler
GET responses may be cached/prefetched, causing unexpected repeated mutations
Use POST route handlers or Server Actions for create/update/delete
next/link prefetch can hit GET routes; a mutating GET will fire on hover
Reserve GET for reads; return data only

root_cause: Storing auth tokens in localStorage exposes them to XSS; use httpOnly cookies instead
fix: Store session/auth tokens in httpOnly, secure, sameSite cookies set by the server
httpOnly cookies are not readable by JavaScript, mitigating token theft via XSS
Set them in a Server Action or Route Handler via cookies().set
Use sameSite='lax' or 'strict' to reduce CSRF exposure
If you must use a token client-side, keep it short-lived and scoped

symptoms: implement role-based UI and route guards
root_cause: Gate UI and routes by role, enforcing authorization on the server in addition to hiding UI
fix: Store roles/permissions on the user record or session claims
In layouts/pages: const { role } = await getSession(); if (role !== 'admin') redirect('/')
Enforce in middleware for whole route groups (e.g. /admin/*)
Always re-check authorization in Server Actions and Route Handlers (UI hiding is not security)
Centralize a can(user, action, resource) helper for consistency
Return 403, not 404, when a logged-in user lacks permission (or 404 to avoid leaking existence)

root_cause: Record an immutable audit trail of sensitive actions for compliance and debugging
fix: Create an audit_log table: actorId, action, entity, entityId, before, after, ip, createdAt
Write the log inside the same DB transaction as the mutation for consistency
Capture actor from the session and IP from x-forwarded-for in the action
Never allow updates/deletes on audit rows (append-only)
Redact secrets/PII before storing diffs
Expose a filtered admin view for review

root_cause: Async Server Components are tested via E2E or by extracting logic; React Testing Library has limited RSC support
fix: Extract data/logic into plain functions and unit test those directly
Use Playwright/E2E to test rendered Server Component output end to end
Vitest can render simple async components but full RSC support is limited
Mock the data layer (services) rather than the component for fast unit tests
Test Client Components with React Testing Library as usual
Test Server Actions by calling the exported function with mocked deps

symptoms: module parse failed for internal package
root_cause: Internal monorepo packages shipped as TypeScript/JSX must be transpiled by the consuming Next.js app
fix: Add the package to next.config.js: transpilePackages: ['@acme/ui']
This compiles the package's TS/JSX through Next's pipeline
Avoid pre-building internal packages just for Next; transpilePackages is simpler
Ensure the package's package.json points main/exports to the source or built files consistently
Restart the dev server after changing the config

root_cause: Serving a Next.js app under a sub-path requires basePath (and sometimes assetPrefix) configuration
fix: Set next.config.js: basePath: '/app' to serve all routes under /app
Internal next/link and router automatically prepend basePath
For external asset CDNs set assetPrefix to the CDN origin
Hardcoded absolute links (href='/foo') must account for basePath or use next/link
API routes also move under basePath; update any external callers

symptoms: JavaScript heap out of memory on Vercel
root_cause: A function processing too much data in memory hits Vercel's memory limit and crashes
fix: Stream large datasets instead of loading them fully into memory
Paginate DB queries; don't SELECT entire large tables
Process files/records in chunks rather than buffering everything
Increase function memory in vercel.json functions config if justified
Check for unbounded accumulation (arrays/maps growing per request)
Move heavy batch processing to a background job/queue

root_cause: Identify the dynamic API forcing a route out of static rendering
fix: Check the build output legend: ○ static, ƒ dynamic, ● SSG
Search the route tree for cookies(), headers(), draftMode(), searchParams usage
Look for fetch with cache: 'no-store' or { next: { revalidate: 0 } }
Check for export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic' or revalidate = 0
Wrap the dynamic part in Suspense to keep the rest static (or use PPR)
Use the 'why is this dynamic' info in the build trace if available

symptoms: mask external API behind same origin
root_cause: Use rewrites to proxy paths to an external backend, avoiding CORS and hiding the origin
fix: next.config.js: async rewrites() { return [{ source: '/api/:path*', destination: 'https://backend.com/:path*' }] }
Rewrites keep the URL the same in the browser (unlike redirects)
Use for gradual migration, BFF proxying, or hiding a backend origin
Add beforeFiles/afterFiles/fallback buckets for ordering control
For auth headers, prefer a Route Handler proxy so secrets stay server-side
Note rewrites run at the edge before route handling

root_cause: Define redirects in next.config for static, build-time URL changes
fix: async redirects() { return [{ source: '/old', destination: '/new', permanent: true }] }
permanent: true => 308 (SEO-preserving); false => 307 (temporary)
Support params: source '/blog/:slug', destination '/posts/:slug'
For dynamic/conditional redirects use middleware instead
Large redirect maps are better stored in Edge Config and applied in middleware
Test that redirect loops aren't created

symptoms: run code once on server start
root_cause: Use instrumentation.ts to run one-time setup (telemetry, warmup) when the server boots
fix: Create instrumentation.ts at the project root exporting async register()
Guard runtime-specific setup: if (process.env.NEXT_RUNTIME === 'nodejs') { ... }
Use it for OTel registration, DB warmup, or feature flag preload
Avoid heavy blocking work; it runs at cold start
In Next 15 it's stable (no experimental flag needed)
Don't rely on it for per-request logic; it runs once per instance

root_cause: Export onRequestError from instrumentation.ts to capture all server-side request errors in one place
fix: In instrumentation.ts export async function onRequestError(error, request, context) { ... }
Forward to Sentry/your logger with request and context metadata
Captures errors from Server Components, Route Handlers, and Server Actions
Pair with error.tsx for the user-facing UI and onRequestError for reporting
Available in Next.js 15

root_cause: Serve private files via short-lived signed URLs instead of making storage public
fix: Store files privately in S3/R2/Supabase Storage (not public)
Generate a presigned GET URL on the server after authorizing the user
Return the signed URL to the client; it expires after minutes
Authorize ownership/permission before signing (don't sign arbitrary keys)
For uploads, issue presigned PUT URLs the same way
Never expose long-lived public URLs for sensitive content

root_cause: next/link prefetches links in the viewport; tune it to balance speed against request volume
fix: Static routes are fully prefetched; dynamic routes prefetch partially by default
Disable for rarely-clicked or expensive links: <Link prefetch={false}>
Excessive prefetch can flood the network on link-heavy pages
Prefetch only happens in production builds, not dev
For programmatic prefetch use router.prefetch(href)
Measure: too many prefetches can compete with critical requests

root_cause: Colocate tests next to the code they cover for discoverability and easy refactors
fix: Place unit tests beside source: Button.tsx + Button.test.tsx
Keep E2E tests in a top-level e2e/ or tests/ directory
Name with .test.ts (unit/integration) and .spec.ts (e2e) consistently
Use __mocks__ adjacent to the module being mocked
Exclude test files from the production build via tsconfig/next config if needed
Mirror the source structure for integration tests that span modules

root_cause: An overly broad middleware matcher runs on static assets and images, adding latency everywhere
fix: Exclude static paths in the matcher: '/((?!_next/static|_next/image|favicon.ico|.*\.(?:png|jpg|svg)$).*)'
Keep middleware logic minimal; it runs on every matched request
Avoid DB calls in middleware (it's edge by default and latency-sensitive)
Use Edge Config for fast config reads instead of network calls
Verify with logs which paths actually hit the middleware

symptoms: when to use edge vs node runtime
root_cause: Choose Edge for low-latency lightweight logic and Node for full APIs and heavy SDKs
fix: Edge: fast global start, but no Node APIs, limited size, no native modules
Node: full Node.js APIs, larger bundles, supports most SDKs (Prisma, etc.)
Use Edge for auth checks, redirects, geolocation, simple JSON, streaming AI
Use Node for DB clients, file processing, libraries needing Node built-ins
Set per route: export const runtime = 'edge' | 'nodejs' (nodejs is default)
Don't put Prisma (non-edge client) or fs-using code on edge

root_cause: Enable typedRoutes for compile-time-checked next/link hrefs and route params
fix: next.config.js: experimental: { typedRoutes: true }
next/link href is now type-checked against existing routes
Invalid or misspelled routes fail at type-check time
Generates a .next/types declaration consumed by TS
Combine with route-specific param typing for safer dynamic links
Requires the App Router

root_cause: Hot reload in dev creates a new PrismaClient per reload, exhausting the connection pool
fix: Cache the client on globalThis in development: globalThis.prisma ??= new PrismaClient()
Only create a fresh client per process in production
Pattern: export const prisma = globalThis.prisma ?? new PrismaClient(); if (env!=='production') globalThis.prisma = prisma
On serverless, use a pooled connection string (PgBouncer/Prisma Accelerate)
Set connection_limit low for serverless to avoid overwhelming the DB

root_cause: AI SDK's streamObject streams a typed object validated against a Zod schema as it generates
fix: Define schema: const schema = z.object({ title: z.string(), tags: z.array(z.string()) })
Server: const result = streamObject({ model, schema, prompt }); return result.toTextStreamResponse()
Client: useObject({ api, schema }) gives a partial object that fills in progressively
Render partial fields as they arrive for a live-typing effect
Validate the final object before persisting
Set a high enough maxDuration for the route

root_cause: Webhook providers retry, so handlers must be idempotent to avoid double-processing
fix: Verify the signature first, then process
Dedupe by event id: store processed ids and skip if already seen
Wrap side effects in a transaction with the idempotency record
Return 2xx quickly; offload heavy work to a queue/background job
On failure return non-2xx so the provider retries
Set a reasonable processing timeout under the function limit

root_cause: Next.js blocks Server Actions when the Origin/Host don't match, common behind a reverse proxy
fix: Set next.config.js: experimental: { serverActions: { allowedOrigins: ['app.example.com'] } }
Ensure the proxy forwards correct Host and X-Forwarded-Host headers
This CSRF protection compares Origin to the allowed hosts
Add all domains the app is served from (custom domain, preview URLs if needed)
Don't disable the protection; configure allowedOrigins instead

root_cause: Gate features by subscription status synced from Stripe webhooks into your database
fix: Sync subscription status to your DB via customer.subscription.* webhooks
Store plan + status + currentPeriodEnd on the user/org record
Check entitlement from your DB on each request (fast, no Stripe call)
Gate UI and enforce on the server in actions/routes
Handle grace periods (past_due) before revoking access
Re-verify against Stripe periodically to catch missed webhooks

symptoms: Vercel function region far from database
function and DB in different regions
root_cause: Co-locate functions with the database; cross-region round trips dominate latency
fix: Set the function region near your DB in vercel.json: { "regions": ["iad1"] }
Or set a per-route preferredRegion: export const preferredRegion = 'iad1'
Match the region to your database's primary region
Edge functions run globally but DB calls still hit one region; pin accordingly
Use read replicas or edge-friendly DBs for global low-latency reads
Measure TTFB by region to confirm the bottleneck

symptoms: Tailwind v4 styles missing after upgrade
root_cause: Tailwind v4 switches to CSS-first config; the old JS config and directives need migrating
fix: Import once in globals.css: @import 'tailwindcss';
Replace @tailwind base/components/utilities with the single @import
Configure theme in CSS via @theme instead of tailwind.config.js (or use @config to keep JS)
Use the new @tailwindcss/postcss plugin in postcss.config
Run the upgrade tool: npx @tailwindcss/upgrade
Restart the dev server after migrating

symptoms: debounce server search with Suspense key
root_cause: Implement server-rendered search-as-you-type by debouncing input into the URL and keying Suspense
fix: Debounce input then router.replace(`?q=${term}`) to update the URL
Read q from the page's searchParams and fetch results on the server
Key the results Suspense boundary on q so it shows a fallback while refetching: <Suspense key={q}>
Use useFormStatus/useTransition to show a pending indicator
Throttle/limit the query and add a minimum length to avoid hammering the DB
Wrap useSearchParams usage in Suspense to avoid client-render bailout

symptoms: how to add Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights
root_cause: Vercel Analytics tracks page views; Speed Insights reports real-user Core Web Vitals
fix: Install: npm install @vercel/analytics @vercel/speed-insights
Add <Analytics /> from @vercel/analytics/react in the root layout
Add <SpeedInsights /> from @vercel/speed-insights/next in the root layout
Enable both in the Vercel dashboard for the project
Speed Insights surfaces LCP/INP/CLS from real users, not lab data
Use the dashboard to find regressions per route after deploys

symptoms: large serializable props from server to client
passing whole DB row to client component
root_cause: Passing large objects across the RSC boundary bloats the payload and may leak fields
fix: Pass only the fields the client component renders, mapped to a minimal DTO
Don't hand entire ORM models (with internal/secret fields) to client components
Heavy read-only rendering should stay in Server Components
Functions and class instances aren't serializable across the boundary
Audit the .rsc payload size in the Network tab

root_cause: Prevent preview/staging deployments from being indexed by search engines
fix: Check the environment: const isProd = process.env.VERCEL_ENV === 'production'
In root layout metadata: robots: isProd ? undefined : { index: false, follow: false }
Also reflect it in app/robots.ts (disallow all when not production)
Optionally add Basic Auth on previews for stronger protection
Verify with view-source that non-prod pages emit <meta name='robots' content='noindex'>

root_cause: Protect cron route handlers so only Vercel's scheduler (with the secret) can invoke them
fix: Set a CRON_SECRET env var in Vercel
In the GET handler verify: request.headers.get('authorization') === `Bearer ${process.env.CRON_SECRET}`
Vercel Cron automatically sends this Authorization header
Return 401 for unauthorized callers
Keep the job idempotent in case of overlapping runs
Log start/finish and duration for observability

root_cause: Group Server Actions by domain in dedicated files marked 'use server' for reuse and testability
fix: Put related actions in src/app/<feature>/actions.ts with 'use server' at top
Or per-function inline 'use server' for one-off actions
Keep actions thin: validate (Zod), authorize, delegate to a service, revalidate
Return typed results for useActionState rather than throwing
Co-locate shared Zod schemas used by both the action and the form
Avoid huge action files; split by feature

symptoms: Text content does not match server-rendered HTML from Date
time renders differently on server and client
root_cause: Rendering Date.now()/new Date()/locale-formatted time differs between server and client, causing hydration mismatch
fix: Render timestamps in a client component after mount (useEffect)
Or format consistently in UTC on both sides with a fixed locale/timezone
Use suppressHydrationWarning on the specific element for intentional time diffs
Avoid Math.random()/Date.now() during render of server components passed to client
Use a stable formatter (date-fns-tz / Intl with explicit timeZone)

root_cause: Serve reads from the nearest replica while routing writes to the primary for global low latency
fix: Provision read replicas in regions near your users (Neon/Supabase/PlanetScale)
Route reads to the closest replica, writes to the primary region
Use a driver/proxy that supports replica routing or branch by query type
Account for replication lag: read-your-writes may need to hit primary after a write
Pin write-heavy functions to the primary region
Measure per-region latency to validate gains

root_cause: next/image requires remote hostnames to be allowlisted; images config is deprecated in favor of remotePatterns
fix: Use remotePatterns: images: { remotePatterns: [{ protocol: 'https', hostname: 'cdn.example.com', pathname: '/uploads/**' }] }
The older images.domains array is deprecated; migrate to remotePatterns
Scope with pathname to limit which paths are allowed
For fully external/unoptimized sources use the unoptimized prop sparingly
Restart dev server after editing the config
Wildcards: hostname '**.example.com' for subdomains

symptoms: handle long-running work off the request
don't block the response on heavy job
root_cause: Offload slow work to a queue/background job so requests return fast and stay within function limits
fix: Return a 202 quickly and process asynchronously (QStash, Inngest, Vercel Cron)
Don't run multi-minute work inside a request handler (timeout/cost risk)
Use waitUntil (after()) for fire-and-forget side effects that may outlive the response
Persist job status so clients can poll or get notified
Make jobs idempotent and retry-safe
Vercel's after() runs code after the response is sent without blocking it

symptoms: run code after response with after()
root_cause: Next.js after() schedules work to run after the response is sent, ideal for logging and analytics
fix: import { after } from 'next/server'
Inside a Server Action/Route Handler: after(async () => { await logEvent(...) })
The response is sent immediately; the callback runs afterward
Use for analytics, audit logs, cache warming, non-critical notifications
Don't use it for work the client needs in the response
Errors in after() won't affect the already-sent response (handle/log them)

symptoms: how to add Clerk auth to Next.js App Router
root_cause: Clerk requires a provider, middleware, and environment keys wired correctly for App Router
fix: Install: npm install @clerk/nextjs
Set NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY in .env.local (and Vercel env vars)
Create middleware.ts at the project root (or src/) exporting clerkMiddleware()
Wrap the root layout's <body> with <ClerkProvider> from @clerk/nextjs
Add <SignInButton>, <SignUpButton>, <UserButton> or build custom pages

root_cause: Middleware matcher does not include the routes that need auth context, so Clerk never attaches the request
fix: Use Clerk's recommended matcher that skips Next internals and static files but includes API routes
config = { matcher: ['/((?!_next|[^?]*\\.(?:html?|css|js(?!on)|jpe?g|png|svg|woff2?)).*)', '/(api|trpc)(.*)'] }
Ensure middleware.ts is at the root or inside src/ if you use a src directory
Do not exclude /api routes if you call auth() in route handlers

symptoms: Property 'userId' does not exist on Promise
root_cause: In @clerk/nextjs v6 auth() and currentUser() became async; calling them synchronously breaks types and runtime
fix: Await it: const { userId } = await auth()
currentUser is also async: const user = await currentUser()
Run the codemod when upgrading from v5: npx @clerk/upgrade
Update any helper functions that returned auth() to be async

root_cause: Need to gate specific routes behind authentication in the App Router
fix: Use createRouteMatcher to define protected paths
const isProtected = createRouteMatcher(['/dashboard(.*)', '/settings(.*)'])
In clerkMiddleware: if (isProtected(req)) await auth.protect()
auth.protect() redirects to the sign-in URL automatically for signed-out users
For API routes return 404/401 instead by checking const { userId } = await auth()

symptoms: read current user in server component
root_cause: Server Components and route handlers need the authenticated user without client hooks
fix: import { auth, currentUser } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'
const { userId } = await auth(); if (!userId) redirect('/sign-in')
Use currentUser() when you need the full user object (email, name, metadata)
auth() is cheap (reads the session token); currentUser() makes a Backend API call

symptoms: use Clerk hooks in client component
root_cause: Client Components need reactive auth/user state for conditional UI
fix: Mark component with 'use client'
const { isLoaded, isSignedIn, user } = useUser()
const { userId, sessionId, getToken } = useAuth()
Guard rendering on isLoaded to avoid flicker before Clerk hydrates
These hooks require the component tree to be inside <ClerkProvider>

root_cause: Publishable or secret key is missing, mismatched (test vs live), or not exposed to the client bundle
fix: NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY must have the NEXT_PUBLIC_ prefix to reach the browser
CLERK_SECRET_KEY is server-only; never prefix it with NEXT_PUBLIC_
Match environments: pk_test_/sk_test_ in development, pk_live_/sk_live_ in production
Add both keys to Vercel project env vars for all environments and redeploy

root_cause: Custom auth pages require catch-all routes and matching env redirect URLs
fix: Create app/sign-in/[[...sign-in]]/page.tsx rendering <SignIn />
Create app/sign-up/[[...sign-up]]/page.tsx rendering <SignUp />
Set NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_URL=/sign-in and NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_UP_URL=/sign-up
Optionally set fallback redirect URLs with NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_FALLBACK_REDIRECT_URL

symptoms: redirect after sign in not working
root_cause: Legacy redirect props/env names were replaced with the fallback/force redirect naming in Core 2
fix: Use forceRedirectUrl / fallbackRedirectUrl props on <SignIn> and <SignUp>
Env equivalents: NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_SIGN_IN_FORCE_REDIRECT_URL
afterSignInUrl and redirectUrl props are deprecated and ignored in v6
fallback applies only when there is no redirect_url in the query; force always wins

root_cause: Clerk delivers user/session events via Svix-signed webhooks that must be verified before trusting the payload
fix: Create a public route handler at app/api/webhooks/clerk/route.ts
npm install svix; verify with new Webhook(CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET).verify(body, headers)
