Read svix-id, svix-timestamp, svix-signature headers and the raw request body (await req.text())
Switch on evt.type: 'user.created', 'user.updated', 'user.deleted' to sync your DB
Add the route to the public matcher so middleware does not block Clerk's request

root_cause: The webhook body was parsed/modified before verification, breaking the Svix signature check
fix: Read the raw body with await req.text(); do NOT use await req.json() first
Pass the exact raw string to wh.verify(payload, headers)
Ensure CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET matches the endpoint's signing secret in the Clerk dashboard
Confirm the route is excluded from auth so Clerk can reach it unauthenticated

root_cause: Server-side admin operations require the Backend API client, not the frontend hooks
fix: import { clerkClient } from '@clerk/nextjs/server'
const client = await clerkClient() (it is async in v6)
await client.users.updateUserMetadata(userId, { publicMetadata: { role: 'admin' } })
await client.users.getUser(userId) / client.users.getUserList()
Only call this on the server; it uses CLERK_SECRET_KEY

root_cause: Clerk has no built-in roles for non-org apps; roles are modeled with metadata and checked server-side
fix: Store role in publicMetadata (readable client+server) or privateMetadata (server only)
Set via Backend API: updateUserMetadata(userId, { publicMetadata: { role } })
Read in middleware/server: const { sessionClaims } = await auth(); sessionClaims?.metadata?.role
Surface metadata in the token via Clerk session token customization to avoid extra API calls
Type it with a global ClerkAuthorization/CustomJwtSessionClaims declaration

root_cause: Multi-tenant apps need the active organization and membership role from the session
fix: Enable Organizations in the Clerk dashboard
const { orgId, orgRole, orgSlug } = await auth()
Use <OrganizationSwitcher /> to let users change active org
Gate data queries by orgId to isolate tenants
Check orgRole === 'org:admin' for org-level permissions

root_cause: currentUser() and clerkClient calls hit Clerk's Backend API, adding latency on every request
fix: Prefer auth() which reads claims from the already-verified session token (no network call)
Customize the session token to include needed fields (role, plan) and read sessionClaims
Cache user lookups with React cache() to dedupe within a request
Only call currentUser() when you truly need fresh profile data

root_cause: Reading custom data from the token avoids Backend API round-trips and enables fast authorization
fix: In Clerk dashboard > Sessions > Customize session token, add e.g. {"metadata": "{{user.public_metadata}}"}
Access via const { sessionClaims } = await auth()
Keep tokens small; only add frequently-needed fields
Declare a CustomJwtSessionClaims interface for type safety
Remember tokens refresh ~every 60s, so changes propagate with a short delay

root_cause: Route handlers must explicitly reject unauthenticated requests rather than redirect
fix: const { userId } = await auth()
if (!userId) return new Response('Unauthorized', { status: 401 })
Proceed with userId-scoped logic afterward
Ensure /api routes are included in the middleware matcher

symptoms: call external API with Clerk token
root_cause: Calling a separate backend needs a short-lived JWT minted by Clerk
fix: Client: const { getToken } = useAuth(); const token = await getToken()
Server action/route: const { getToken } = await auth(); const token = await getToken({ template: 'my-template' })
Send it as Authorization: Bearer <token>
Create a JWT template in the dashboard if the backend expects specific claims

root_cause: Trusting a client-supplied user identifier lets attackers act as other users
fix: Never accept userId from the request body/query for authorization
Always derive identity server-side via const { userId } = await auth()
Scope all DB queries to the server-derived userId/orgId
Validate ownership before mutating any record

symptoms: debug why Clerk thinks user is signed out
root_cause: Signed-out state usually comes from middleware not running, cookie/domain mismatch, or key environment mismatch
fix: Confirm middleware.ts location and matcher actually cover the route
Check the __session cookie exists in devtools (correct domain, not blocked)
Verify publishable/secret keys are the same instance and environment
On custom domains, ensure the Clerk instance's allowed origins include it
Look for SDK version mismatch between @clerk packages

root_cause: Production instances require DNS records and live keys distinct from the development instance
fix: Create a Production instance in the Clerk dashboard
Add the CNAME records Clerk provides (clerk, accounts, etc.) at your DNS provider
Swap to pk_live_/sk_live_ keys in Vercel production env vars
Configure the production OAuth credentials (your own, not Clerk's shared dev ones)
Wait for DNS/SSL to verify before switching traffic

root_cause: Rendering auth-dependent UI before Clerk loads causes flicker/hydration mismatch
fix: Gate UI on isLoaded from useUser/useAuth before rendering
Use <SignedIn> and <SignedOut> control components which handle the loading state
For server-rendered gating, decide in a Server Component using auth() instead of client hooks
Avoid conditionally rendering based on isSignedIn before isLoaded is true

root_cause: Custom UIs need to trigger sign-out without the prebuilt UserButton
fix: Client: const { signOut } = useClerk(); onClick={() => signOut({ redirectUrl: '/' })}
Or use the <SignOutButton> component
signOut clears the session and cookies, then redirects
For all-device sign out, revoke sessions via the Backend API

root_cause: Server Actions run on the server and can authenticate directly via Clerk
fix: 'use server' at the top of the action file/function
const { userId } = await auth(); if (!userId) throw new Error('Unauthorized')
Perform the userId-scoped mutation and revalidatePath afterward
Never trust hidden form fields for identity

root_cause: Prebuilt components need the appearance prop or a theme to match the app design
fix: Pass appearance to <ClerkProvider> or individual components
Use @clerk/themes: import { dark } from '@clerk/themes'; appearance={{ baseTheme: dark }}
Override variables: appearance={{ variables: { colorPrimary: '#6c47ff' } }}
Target element keys via appearance.elements for fine-grained CSS

root_cause: A protected sign-in route or mismatched redirect URLs cause the middleware to bounce endlessly
fix: Ensure /sign-in and /sign-up are NOT matched as protected routes
auth.protect() should only run on app routes, not the auth pages themselves
Verify SIGN_IN_URL env matches the actual route path
Check that fallback/force redirect URLs are not pointing back to a protected page that redirects again

root_cause: Mixing development and production Clerk instances leaks data and breaks auth
fix: Use the development instance only for local/preview, production instance for live
Store keys per Vercel environment (Development/Preview/Production)
Never commit keys; rely on .env.local locally and Vercel env vars in cloud
Rotate CLERK_SECRET_KEY if it is ever exposed

root_cause: Fine-grained org permissions/roles are checked with the has() helper
fix: const { has } = await auth()
if (!has({ permission: 'org:invoices:create' })) return forbidden()
Or check role: has({ role: 'org:admin' })
Client equivalent: const { has } = useAuth()
Define permissions under organization roles in the dashboard

symptoms: restrict sign ups to a domain
root_cause: Open sign-up needs restrictions for B2B or invite-only apps
fix: Use Clerk dashboard > User & Authentication > Restrictions for allowlist/blocklist
Enable invitation-only mode and send invites via the Backend API (createInvitation)
For org apps, disable personal accounts and require an org membership
Optionally enforce allowed email domains

symptoms: Clerk middleware src directory not found
root_cause: Next.js only picks up middleware at the project root or inside src/ when using a src directory
fix: Place middleware.ts at the repo root OR at src/middleware.ts (matching your app dir location)
Do not nest it inside app/ — it will be ignored
Restart the dev server after moving it
Confirm the file default/named export is clerkMiddleware()

root_cause: clerkMiddleware runs on every matched request, so its runtime affects latency globally
fix: clerkMiddleware is edge-compatible and fast by default
Keep middleware logic minimal — only route matching and auth.protect()
Avoid heavy Backend API calls (currentUser) inside middleware
Narrow the matcher so static assets never trigger middleware

root_cause: Several configuration items are easy to miss before shipping Clerk to production
fix: Production instance created with DNS/SSL verified
Live keys set in Vercel production env vars
Own OAuth credentials configured for each social provider
Webhook endpoint registered with the correct signing secret
Redirect URLs and sign-in/up URL envs set
Restrictions/allowlist configured if invite-only

root_cause: Custom token claims are untyped by default, losing type safety on sessionClaims
fix: Create a types/globals.d.ts file
declare global { interface CustomJwtSessionClaims { metadata: { role?: 'admin' | 'user' } } }
Now const { sessionClaims } = await auth() infers metadata.role
Keep the declaration in sync with the dashboard token customization

root_cause: Inviting users programmatically requires the Backend API
fix: const client = await clerkClient()
await client.invitations.createInvitation({ emailAddress, redirectUrl, publicMetadata })
User receives an email; on accept they are created with the metadata you set
List/revoke via client.invitations.getInvitationList()/revokeInvitation()

symptoms: difference between auth and currentUser Clerk
root_cause: Choosing the wrong helper adds unnecessary latency or misses data
fix: auth(): synchronous-cost token read; returns userId, sessionClaims, orgId, getToken — no network call
currentUser(): async Backend API call; returns the full User object (email, names, metadata)
Use auth() for authorization checks and IDs
Use currentUser() only when rendering profile data

root_cause: Newer @clerk/nextjs starts in keyless mode without env keys, showing a claim banner
fix: Create the application in the Clerk dashboard and copy real keys
Add NEXT_PUBLIC_CLERK_PUBLISHABLE_KEY and CLERK_SECRET_KEY to .env.local
Restart the dev server to leave keyless mode
Keyless mode is dev-only and must not be relied on for production

symptoms: conditional UI signed in signed out
show login button when logged out
root_cause: Declarative auth-gated UI without manual loading checks
fix: import { SignedIn, SignedOut, UserButton, SignInButton } from '@clerk/nextjs'
<SignedOut><SignInButton /></SignedOut>
<SignedIn><UserButton /></SignedIn>
These components handle the loading state internally to avoid flicker

root_cause: Loading Clerk client SDK everywhere can add to the client bundle and TTI
fix: Keep <ClerkProvider> at the root but render auth UI lazily where possible
Prefer server-side auth() checks over client hooks when no interactivity is needed
Use control components instead of bespoke client logic
Avoid importing @clerk/nextjs into purely static pages

root_cause: Plan-based feature gating can be checked with has() when Clerk Billing is enabled
fix: Enable Billing and define plans/features in the dashboard
Check access: const { has } = await auth(); has({ plan: 'pro' })
Or feature-level: has({ feature: 'advanced_analytics' })
Use <PricingTable /> for a prebuilt checkout UI
Combine with org context for B2B plans

root_cause: Apps with several protected areas need a single matcher covering all of them
fix: const isProtected = createRouteMatcher(['/dashboard(.*)', '/account(.*)', '/api/private(.*)'])
if (isProtected(req)) await auth.protect()
Use (.*) suffixes to cover nested segments
Keep public routes (marketing, sign-in) out of the matcher

root_cause: Social providers must be enabled and, for production, use your own OAuth credentials
fix: Dashboard > User & Authentication > Social Connections, toggle the provider
Development uses Clerk's shared credentials automatically
For production, register an OAuth app with the provider and paste client ID/secret
Set the authorized redirect URL to your Clerk accounts domain
<SignIn /> renders the enabled providers automatically

symptoms: social login works in dev not production Clerk
root_cause: Production uses your own OAuth credentials; missing/incorrect ones break social login after going live
fix: Create a real OAuth app with each provider for production
Enter client ID/secret in the provider's settings in the Clerk dashboard
Set the redirect URI to https://accounts.<your-domain>/v1/oauth_callback (per dashboard)
Do not rely on Clerk's shared dev credentials in production

root_cause: Profile mutations from your backend go through the Backend API client
fix: const client = await clerkClient()
await client.users.updateUser(userId, { firstName, lastName })
Metadata: updateUserMetadata(userId, { publicMetadata, privateMetadata })
publicMetadata is exposed to the frontend; privateMetadata stays server-side only
unsafeMetadata can be set by the user from the client — validate it

root_cause: publicMetadata and unsafeMetadata are readable by the client and must never hold secrets
fix: Use privateMetadata for server-only data (Stripe customer IDs, internal flags)
Use publicMetadata only for non-sensitive display/role data
Treat unsafeMetadata as untrusted user input — validate before use
Never store API keys, tokens, or PII you would not show the user in public/unsafe metadata

root_cause: Missing webhook events stem from unregistered endpoints, blocked routes, or filtered event types
fix: Confirm the endpoint URL is registered in dashboard > Webhooks and is publicly reachable
Ensure the route is in the public matcher (middleware not blocking it)
Check the subscribed event types include the ones you expect
Use the dashboard's 'Send test event' and inspect delivery attempts/logs
Verify your handler returns 2xx quickly (Svix retries on non-2xx)

root_cause: Webhooks need a public URL, so local testing requires a tunnel
fix: Run a tunnel: ngrok http 3000 (or use the Vercel preview URL)
Register the tunnel URL + /api/webhooks/clerk in the dashboard
Copy the endpoint's signing secret into CLERK_WEBHOOK_SECRET
Trigger by creating a test user; watch your terminal logs

symptoms: dedupe currentUser calls in one request
root_cause: Several components calling currentUser() in one render trigger duplicate Backend API calls
fix: Wrap it: const getUser = cache(async () => await currentUser())
Import cache from 'react'
All components in the same request share one result
Prefer passing the user down from a layout/page over re-fetching

root_cause: Preview deployments use ephemeral domains that must be allowed by the Clerk instance
fix: Development instance accepts any localhost/preview origin by default
For production-instance previews, configure allowed origins or use the dev instance for previews
Set keys per Vercel environment so previews use development keys
Avoid hardcoding the domain in redirect URLs; use relative paths

root_cause: Org member management uses the Backend API scoped to an organization
fix: const client = await clerkClient()
await client.organizations.getOrganizationMembershipList({ organizationId })
Add: createOrganizationMembership({ organizationId, userId, role })
Update role: updateOrganizationMembership(...)
Always verify the caller's org role before mutating membership

symptoms: clerkMiddleware auth is not a function
root_cause: Code still uses the removed authMiddleware API instead of clerkMiddleware
fix: Replace authMiddleware() with clerkMiddleware((auth, req) => { ... })
Inside, auth is a callable: const { userId } = await auth()
Protect via await auth.protect()
publicRoutes/ignoredRoutes options are gone — use createRouteMatcher instead

symptoms: scope database rows to Clerk user
root_cause: Every user-owned row should carry the Clerk userId (and orgId) for isolation
fix: Add a clerkUserId column (and organizationId for org apps)
Set it from server-derived auth(), never from client input
Filter every query by the authenticated userId/orgId
On user.deleted webhook, clean up or anonymize their rows

root_cause: generateMetadata runs on the server and can use Clerk to personalize head tags
fix: export async function generateMetadata() { const { userId } = await auth(); ... }
Return a title/description based on the user or fall back for signed-out
Avoid currentUser() here unless necessary (latency on every render)
Do not expose private data in public metadata tags

symptoms: Clerk: auth() was called but Clerk can't detect usage of clerkMiddleware
root_cause: auth() requires clerkMiddleware to have run for the request; missing/mismatched middleware breaks it
fix: Add clerkMiddleware() in middleware.ts and ensure the matcher covers the route
Place middleware at the project root or src/ correctly
For the matcher, include API/trpc routes that call auth()
Restart the dev server after adding/moving middleware

root_cause: User deletion must propagate from Clerk to your database via webhooks
fix: Subscribe to the user.deleted event
In the handler, delete/anonymize rows where clerkUserId === evt.data.id
To delete from your side too: clerkClient().users.deleteUser(userId)
Return 2xx so Svix does not retry

symptoms: where to put Clerk auth checks
root_cause: Defense-in-depth means auth is enforced at multiple layers, not only middleware
fix: Middleware: coarse route gating with auth.protect() for UX redirects
Server Components/actions/route handlers: authoritative checks via auth() before data access
Never rely on middleware alone for data security — it can be bypassed for direct API calls if misconfigured
Scope every query to the authenticated identity

symptoms: force token refresh after metadata change
root_cause: Session tokens cache claims for ~60s, so metadata changes do not appear instantly
fix: Accept up to ~1 minute of propagation delay for token-embedded claims
For immediate effect, read fresh data via currentUser()/Backend API instead of the token
Client: await user.reload() to refresh the user object after changes
Avoid embedding rapidly-changing data in the session token

root_cause: tRPC needs the Clerk identity injected into its request context
fix: In createContext: const { userId } = await auth(); return { userId }
Make a protectedProcedure middleware that throws TRPCError UNAUTHORIZED if !ctx.userId
Include /api/trpc in the Clerk middleware matcher
Scope queries/mutations by ctx.userId

symptoms: do not fetch user in middleware
root_cause: Calling Backend API helpers in middleware adds latency to every matched request
fix: Middleware should only do route matching and auth.protect()/auth() token reads
Move user data fetching into the page/route handler where it is needed
Read role/plan from sessionClaims in middleware instead of currentUser()
Keep the matcher tight to reduce invocations

root_cause: Global CSS or Tailwind base styles can clash with Clerk component internals
fix: Style via the appearance prop (elements/variables), not by targeting Clerk's internal class names
Internal class names are not stable across versions
Use appearance.elements keys documented by Clerk for safe overrides
Scope aggressive global resets so they don't bleed into Clerk components

root_cause: Teams must weigh managed UX/features against self-hosted control and cost
fix: Clerk: hosted UI components, orgs, MFA, webhooks, billing — fastest to ship, usage-based pricing
Auth.js/NextAuth: self-hosted, free, full control, more wiring and maintenance
Pick Clerk for B2B/orgs, rich account UX, and speed
Pick Auth.js when you need full data ownership or want to avoid per-MAU cost

root_cause: Redirecting on isSignedIn before Clerk loads causes false sign-out redirects
fix: const { isLoaded, isSignedIn } = useAuth()
if (!isLoaded) return <Spinner />
Only act on isSignedIn once isLoaded is true
Prefer server-side gating (auth() + redirect) to avoid client flashes entirely

root_cause: MFA is a dashboard toggle plus optional enforcement
fix: Dashboard > User & Authentication > Multi-factor, enable TOTP/SMS/backup codes
Users manage MFA in <UserProfile /> / <UserButton> account settings
Enforce step-up for sensitive actions via reverification
MFA state is reflected in the session

root_cause: Sensitive operations should require fresh authentication even within a valid session
fix: Wrap protected logic and check the user's reverification status
Use auth.protect with reverification options or Clerk's reverification helpers
Prompt the user to re-enter credentials/MFA before the action proceeds
Apply to actions like changing email, deleting account, or payments

root_cause: Prebuilt components default to English and need a localization object
fix: npm install @clerk/localizations
import { frFR } from '@clerk/localizations'
<ClerkProvider localization={frFR}>
Override individual strings by passing a custom localization object

root_cause: Clerk SDK calls can throw typed errors that should be handled, not swallowed
fix: Wrap Backend API calls in try/catch
Use isClerkAPIResponseError(err) to detect Clerk errors and read err.errors[].message
Map known codes to friendly messages for the UI
Log unexpected errors with context (userId, action) for debugging

symptoms: setup TanStack Query Next.js App Router
root_cause: App Router needs a client-only provider with a stable QueryClient that is not shared across requests on the server
fix: npm install @tanstack/react-query
Create app/providers.tsx with 'use client' exporting <QueryClientProvider client={queryClient}>
Make the QueryClient with useState(() => new QueryClient()) so it is created once per client mount
Wrap children in the root layout with <Providers>
Optionally add <ReactQueryDevtools /> in development

root_cause: Creating the QueryClient inline in the component body makes a new cache on every render
fix: Use lazy state: const [client] = useState(() => new QueryClient())
Or create a module-level singleton for the browser and a fresh one per request on the server
Never write const client = new QueryClient() directly in the component body
A stable client preserves cache across re-renders

symptoms: shared QueryClient on server leaks data between users
root_cause: A module-level QueryClient on the server is shared across all requests, leaking one user's data to another
fix: On the server, create a new QueryClient per request (never a global)
Use a make-query-client helper that returns a browser singleton but a fresh server instance
if (isServer) return makeQueryClient(); else return (browserClient ??= makeQueryClient())
This is critical for prefetching/dehydration in RSC

root_cause: Fetching with caching/loading/error handled by the library
fix: const { data, isPending, isError, error } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos'], queryFn: fetchTodos })
queryKey uniquely identifies and caches the query
queryFn must return a promise and throw on error (so isError works)

Render: if (isPending) return ; if (isError) return

{error.message}

root_cause: TanStack Query v5 renamed several fields; isLoading semantics changed
fix: Use isPending for 'no data yet' (was isLoading in v4)
isLoading now means isPending && isFetching (first load in flight)
cacheTime was renamed to gcTime
useMutation: isLoading became isPending too
Run the v5 codemod: npx @tanstack/query-codemod

root_cause: Parameterized queries must include their inputs in the queryKey for correct caching
fix: useQuery({ queryKey: ['todo', id], queryFn: () => fetchTodo(id) })
Include every variable the queryFn depends on in the key
Keys are compared structurally (deep equality), order of object keys does not matter
Use array keys from generic to specific: ['todos', { status, page }]

root_cause: Scattered inline keys cause typos and inconsistent invalidation
fix: Create a key factory: const todoKeys = { all: ['todos'], list: (f) => [...todoKeys.all, 'list', f], detail: (id) => [...todoKeys.all, 'detail', id] }
Invalidate broadly with queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: todoKeys.all })
Co-locate keys with their queries/feature
Consider @lukemorales/query-key-factory for larger apps

root_cause: Misunderstanding stale vs garbage-collection time leads to too many or too few refetches
fix: staleTime: how long data is considered fresh; fresh data is served without refetching (default 0)
gcTime: how long unused/inactive cache is kept before garbage collection (default 5 min)
Stale data still shows instantly, then refetches in the background on triggers
Raise staleTime to reduce refetches for data that rarely changes

root_cause: Default staleTime of 0 means data is immediately stale and refetches on every trigger
fix: Set a sensible staleTime per query (e.g. staleTime: 60_000)
Disable refetchOnWindowFocus where not needed
Set global defaults in QueryClient defaultOptions.queries
Understand triggers: mount, window focus, reconnect, key change

root_cause: Mutations handle create/update/delete and trigger cache updates on success
fix: const m = useMutation({ mutationFn: createTodo, onSuccess: () => queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }) })
Trigger with m.mutate(payload) or await m.mutateAsync(payload)
Read m.isPending for button loading state
invalidateQueries in onSuccess refetches affected lists

root_cause: Optimistic updates make the UI feel instant by writing the cache before the server confirms
fix: onMutate: cancel in-flight queries (cancelQueries), snapshot previous, setQueryData optimistically, return context
onError: roll back with queryClient.setQueryData(key, context.previous)
onSettled: invalidateQueries to reconcile with the server
Always cancel related queries first to avoid overwriting the optimistic value

root_cause: After a mutation, related queries must be invalidated so the UI reflects new data
fix: queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: ['todos'] }) marks matching queries stale and refetches active ones
Partial keys match broadly: ['todos'] invalidates ['todos', 'list', ...] too
Use exact: true to match only the precise key
Return the promise from onSuccess to keep the mutation pending until refetch completes (optional)

root_cause: Infinite lists use useInfiniteQuery with cursor/page params
fix: useInfiniteQuery({ queryKey: ['feed'], queryFn: ({ pageParam }) => fetchPage(pageParam), initialPageParam: 0, getNextPageParam: (last) => last.nextCursor })
Render data.pages.flatMap(p => p.items)
Call fetchNextPage() when the sentinel enters view (IntersectionObserver)
Guard with hasNextPage and isFetchingNextPage

root_cause: Page changes flash a loading state unless previous data is kept during fetch
fix: import { keepPreviousData } from '@tanstack/react-query'
useQuery({ queryKey: ['todos', page], queryFn, placeholderData: keepPreviousData })
isPlaceholderData tells you the shown data is from the previous page
In v5 keepPreviousData replaced the old keepPreviousData: true option

root_cause: Queries that need data from another query must wait until that data exists
fix: const { data: user } = useQuery({ queryKey: ['user'], queryFn })
useQuery({ queryKey: ['projects', user?.id], queryFn: () => fetchProjects(user.id), enabled: !!user?.id })
enabled: false keeps the query idle until the condition is true
While disabled, status is 'pending' with fetchStatus 'idle'

root_cause: Server prefetching avoids client loading spinners by dehydrating state into the client
fix: In a Server Component: const qc = getQueryClient(); await qc.prefetchQuery({ queryKey, queryFn })
Wrap the client child: <HydrationBoundary state={dehydrate(qc)}><ClientList/></HydrationBoundary>
The client useQuery with the same key hydrates instantly, no loading state
Use a per-request server QueryClient (never a global)

root_cause: useSuspenseQuery integrates with React Suspense so data is always defined in the component
fix: const { data } = useSuspenseQuery({ queryKey, queryFn }) — data is non-nullable
Wrap the component in <Suspense fallback={...}> (e.g. via loading.tsx)
Pair with an <ErrorBoundary> for error states
Do not use enabled with suspense queries; use a regular useQuery for conditional fetching

root_cause: v5 removed onSuccess/onError/onSettled callbacks from useQuery (they remain on useMutation)
fix: Move side effects out of useQuery callbacks
Derive state during render or use useEffect keyed on data
For global handling, use the QueryCache onError in QueryClient config
Mutation callbacks (onSuccess/onError) still work

root_cause: Per-query error handling is repetitive; centralize it on the cache
fix: new QueryClient({ queryCache: new QueryCache({ onError: (err) => toast.error(err.message) }) })
Same for MutationCache onError
Keep component-level handling for specific UX
Throw typed errors from queryFn so messages are meaningful

root_cause: App-wide query behavior is configured once on the QueryClient
fix: new QueryClient({ defaultOptions: { queries: { staleTime: 60_000, retry: 1, refetchOnWindowFocus: false } } })
Override per-query as needed
Set mutation defaults under defaultOptions.mutations
Tune retry to avoid hammering a failing endpoint

root_cause: Default retries (3, exponential backoff) can amplify load against a broken endpoint
fix: Set retry to a number or a function: retry: (count, err) => err.status >= 500 && count < 2
Do not retry 4xx (client) errors — they will not succeed
Tune retryDelay for backoff
Disable retries for mutations that are not idempotent

root_cause: After a mutation you can patch the cache directly to avoid a network refetch
fix: queryClient.setQueryData(['todo', id], (old) => ({ ...old, ...updates }))
For lists: setQueryData(['todos'], (old) => old.map(t => t.id === id ? next : t))
Use the updater function form to base on the latest cache
Combine with invalidateQueries on a longer cadence to stay consistent

root_cause: select lets you subscribe to a slice/transform without re-rendering on unrelated changes
fix: useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn, select: (data) => data.items.length })
The component only re-renders when the selected output changes
Keep select referentially stable (define outside or useCallback) for object outputs
Great for computing derived data from a shared query

symptoms: query not refetching when variable changes
root_cause: If a queryFn input is not in the queryKey, changing it will not refetch
fix: Include every dependency in the queryKey: ['search', term, filters]
Changing the key creates/serves a different cache entry
Do not read changing values from closure without keying them
Treat queryKey like a dependency array

root_cause: Search inputs should debounce the term before it becomes a query key
fix: Debounce the input value (e.g. useDebounce) and use the debounced value in the key
useQuery({ queryKey: ['search', debounced], queryFn, enabled: debounced.length > 1 })
placeholderData: keepPreviousData to avoid flicker between terms
staleTime keeps recent searches cached

root_cause: Devtools visualize cache state, fetch status, and stale timers
fix: npm install @tanstack/react-query-devtools
Render <ReactQueryDevtools initialIsOpen={false} /> inside the provider
It is automatically excluded from production builds
Use it to inspect keys, data, and why something is/ isn't refetching

symptoms: using useEffect to fetch instead of react-query
root_cause: Hand-rolled useEffect fetching reinvents caching, dedup, and race handling that the library already solves
fix: Replace useEffect+useState fetching with useQuery
You get caching, dedup, background refetch, retries, and request cancellation for free
Avoid storing server data in useState/Redux — keep it in the query cache
Use mutations + invalidation instead of manual refetch flags

symptoms: do not duplicate server data in redux
root_cause: Conflating server cache with UI state causes sync bugs and boilerplate
fix: Server state (fetched, async, shared) → TanStack Query
Client/UI state (modals, form inputs, toggles) → useState/Zustand/Context
Do not copy query data into another store; read it from the cache
Derive, don't duplicate

root_cause: Long requests should be abortable to avoid wasted work and races
fix: queryFn receives a context with signal: queryFn: ({ signal }) => fetch(url, { signal })
TanStack Query aborts the signal when the query is no longer needed
Pass the signal to fetch/axios so in-flight requests cancel
cancelQueries() also aborts ongoing fetches

root_cause: Near-real-time data can be polled with refetchInterval
fix: useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn, refetchInterval: 5000 })
Use a function to poll conditionally: refetchInterval: (q) => q.state.data?.done ? false : 2000
refetchIntervalInBackground keeps polling when the tab is hidden (use sparingly)
For true real-time, prefer websockets + setQueryData over aggressive polling

root_cause: Prefetching on intent (hover/focus) makes navigation feel instant
fix: onMouseEnter={() => queryClient.prefetchQuery({ queryKey: ['post', id], queryFn })}
Set a staleTime so the prefetch isn't immediately considered stale
Combine with Next.js <Link prefetch> for route + data prefetch
Avoid over-prefetching to save bandwidth

root_cause: Non-serializable data (Dates, Maps) or unstable shapes break dehydration/hydration
fix: Ensure queryFn returns JSON-serializable data for dehydrated queries
Convert Dates to ISO strings server-side and parse on the client (or use a custom serializer)
Use the same queryKey on server prefetch and client useQuery
Provide a stable QueryClient per request on the server

root_cause: Strong typing comes from the queryFn return type, no generics needed
fix: Type the queryFn: const fetchTodos = (): Promise<Todo[]> => ...
useQuery infers data as Todo[] | undefined
error defaults to Error; register a global Error type via Register interface if needed
Avoid passing explicit generics; let inference flow from queryFn

root_cause: Wrapping queries in feature hooks centralizes keys, fns, and options
fix: export function useTodos(filters) { return useQuery({ queryKey: todoKeys.list(filters), queryFn: () => api.todos.list(filters) }) }
Components call useTodos() without knowing keys/endpoints
Co-locate the hook, key factory, and API call
Easier to test and refactor

root_cause: queryOptions creates a typed, reusable config shared between useQuery, prefetch, and setQueryData
fix: const todoOptions = (id) => queryOptions({ queryKey: ['todo', id], queryFn: () => fetchTodo(id) })
Client: useQuery(todoOptions(id))
Server: queryClient.prefetchQuery(todoOptions(id))
Ensures the key and fn never drift between call sites

root_cause: TanStack Query uses structural sharing so unchanged parts keep their reference, minimizing re-renders
fix: Rely on built-in structural sharing — identical data keeps the same reference
Use select to subscribe to only the slice you need
Avoid mapping/cloning data in render (breaks referential equality downstream)
tracked properties mean a component only re-renders for fields it actually reads

root_cause: Two ways to show data before a fetch resolves, with different cache semantics
fix: initialData: persisted to cache and treated as real (respects staleTime) — use for known-good data
placeholderData: shown but not cached, always refetches — use for temporary skeletons/previous page
initialDataUpdatedAt lets you control staleness of seeded data
Use keepPreviousData (a placeholderData helper) for pagination

root_cause: A disabled query (enabled: false) reports status 'pending' with fetchStatus 'idle' — it is waiting, not loading
fix: Distinguish status (pending/error/success) from fetchStatus (fetching/paused/idle)
If enabled is false, the query intentionally does not run
Check your enabled condition resolves truthy
Use isLoading (pending && fetching) to show spinners only when actually fetching

root_cause: Fetching a dynamic number of resources in parallel uses useQueries
fix: const results = useQueries({ queries: ids.map(id => ({ queryKey: ['item', id], queryFn: () => fetchItem(id) })) })
results is an array of query results, one per id
Use combine to aggregate: useQueries({ queries, combine: (r) => r.map(x => x.data) })
For a fixed small number, just call multiple useQuery hooks

root_cause: Updating an infinite query's cache requires editing the pages structure
fix: setQueryData(['feed'], (old) => ({ ...old, pages: old.pages.map((p,i) => i===0 ? { ...p, items: [newItem, ...p.items] } : p) }))
Remember the data shape is { pages: [], pageParams: [] }
invalidateQueries on settle to re-sync ordering/pagination
Snapshot and roll back on error as with normal optimistic updates

root_cause: Without staleTime, prefetched-then-hydrated data is instantly stale and refetches on mount, wasting the prefetch
fix: Set a staleTime (e.g. 60s) on prefetched queries so the client doesn't immediately refetch
Set it via defaultOptions or per query in queryOptions
This makes server prefetch actually save a request
Balance freshness vs avoiding the double fetch

root_cause: Imperative cache operations need the active QueryClient instance
fix: const queryClient = useQueryClient() inside components
Use it for invalidateQueries, setQueryData, getQueryData, prefetchQuery, cancelQueries
Outside React, pass the same client instance explicitly
Do not new up another QueryClient — that has a separate cache

root_cause: Very long gcTime with many unique keys keeps inactive data in memory
fix: Default gcTime is 5 minutes for inactive queries — usually fine
Lower gcTime for high-cardinality keys (search terms, per-id) to free memory
Avoid creating unbounded unique query keys
Remove specific queries with queryClient.removeQueries when truly done

root_cause: Caching across reloads/offline uses the persistence plugin
fix: npm install @tanstack/query-sync-storage-persister @tanstack/react-query-persist-client
Create a persister with window.localStorage
Wrap app in <PersistQueryClientProvider persistOptions={{ persister }}>
Set a buster/maxAge to invalidate stale persisted cache between deploys

root_cause: Calling mutate during render (instead of in an event handler) fires it repeatedly
fix: Call mutate only inside event handlers/effects, never in the render body
Disable the submit button while m.isPending
For idempotency, dedupe server-side with an idempotency key
Use mutateAsync + await in handlers, not at module/render scope

root_cause: Understanding refetch triggers prevents both stale UIs and excessive requests
fix: A stale query refetches on: component mount, window refocus, network reconnect, and queryKey change
Fresh (within staleTime) queries do not refetch on those triggers
refetchInterval adds time-based refetching
Manual: refetch() or invalidateQueries()

root_cause: Cached queries make back-navigation instant since data is served from cache
fix: Keep a reasonable staleTime/gcTime so revisited pages render from cache
useQuery on the list page returns cached data immediately, then background-refetches if stale
Combine with Next.js prefetch and keepPreviousData for smooth UX
Avoid removeQueries on navigate unless you intend to drop the cache

root_cause: A few defaults are worth reviewing before production
fix: Per-request QueryClient on the server; singleton in the browser
Sensible global staleTime and retry configured
Global error handler (QueryCache/MutationCache onError)
Query key factory for consistent invalidation
staleTime set on prefetched queries to avoid double fetch
Devtools excluded from production (automatic)

root_cause: A refresh button should trigger an explicit refetch without changing the key
fix: const { refetch, isFetching } = useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn })
<button onClick={() => refetch()} disabled={isFetching}>Refresh</button>
Or invalidateQueries({ queryKey }) to refresh everything matching
isFetching (not isPending) reflects background refetches

symptoms: queryFn must throw to be error
root_cause: fetch() does not reject on HTTP errors, so react-query sees them as success unless you throw
fix: Check res.ok in the queryFn and throw new Error on non-2xx
if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`HTTP ${res.status}`)
Only then does isError/error populate
axios rejects on errors automatically; fetch does not

root_cause: Errors can be routed to an Error Boundary instead of handled inline
fix: useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn, throwOnError: true })
Wrap the subtree in a React <ErrorBoundary>
Use a function: throwOnError: (err) => err.status >= 500 to only escalate server errors
useSuspenseQuery throws by default

symptoms: function or class in query key
root_cause: Query keys must be deterministic and serializable for hashing and dedup
fix: Use only JSON-serializable values in keys (strings, numbers, plain objects, arrays)
Do not put functions, class instances, or unstable objects in keys
Object key order does not matter (hashed deterministically)
Avoid Date objects in keys; use timestamps/ISO strings

root_cause: Server Actions can serve as mutationFns, combining RSC mutations with client cache updates
fix: mutationFn: async (input) => await myServerAction(input)
onSuccess: invalidateQueries or setQueryData to update the client cache
The server action does the auth + DB write; react-query manages client state
You can also call revalidatePath inside the action for RSC data

root_cause: Refetch-on-focus is great for dashboards but jarring for forms/static data
fix: Globally: defaultOptions.queries.refetchOnWindowFocus: false
Per query if only some should refetch on focus
Keep it on for data that must be fresh (prices, notifications)
Combine with a staleTime so fresh data won't refetch on focus anyway

symptoms: meta field on query for context
root_cause: meta attaches static context read by global cache callbacks
fix: useQuery({ queryKey, queryFn, meta: { errorMessage: 'Failed to load todos' } })
Read it in QueryCache onError: (err, query) => toast(query.meta?.errorMessage)
Useful for per-query toast text or analytics tags
meta is not part of the cache key

symptoms: cannot read property of undefined query
root_cause: data is undefined until the first successful fetch, causing runtime errors if accessed unguarded
fix: Guard with isPending or check data before access
Provide initialData/placeholderData to have a defined shape
Or use useSuspenseQuery where data is always defined
Use optional chaining and sensible defaults (data?.items ?? [])

symptoms: reset or clear cache on logout
root_cause: On logout, user-specific cached data must be cleared to prevent leakage
fix: queryClient.clear() removes all queries and mutations from the cache
Or selectively removeQueries for user-scoped keys
Call it in your sign-out handler before redirect
Pair with Clerk/Auth.js signOut to fully reset state

root_cause: networkMode controls how queries behave without a connection
fix: Default 'online': queries pause (fetchStatus 'paused') when offline and resume on reconnect
'always': run regardless of connection (good for non-HTTP queryFns)
'offlineFirst': try once then pause — useful with HTTP caching/service workers
Set per query or in defaultOptions

root_cause: Queries are easiest to maintain when colocated with the feature that uses them
fix: Keep the key factory, query hook, and API call in the feature folder
Export a useFeatureData() hook rather than raw useQuery in components
Share queryOptions between server prefetch and client hooks
Avoid a giant central 'queries.ts' that every feature imports from

root_cause: Unexpected fetches usually come from key instability, staleTime 0, or focus/mount triggers
fix: Open Devtools and watch the query's observers and fetch status
Check the queryKey is referentially stable across renders (no new objects each render)
Verify staleTime — default 0 means every trigger refetches
Inspect refetchOnWindowFocus/refetchOnMount settings
Look for a parent remounting the component

symptoms: mutation loading and error states UI
root_cause: Mutations expose status flags for building responsive forms
fix: const { mutate, isPending, isError, error, isSuccess } = useMutation({ mutationFn })
<button disabled={isPending}>{isPending ? 'Saving…' : 'Save'}</button>

root_cause: Sometimes you need the current cached value outside of a query subscription

Use getQueriesData(['todos']) to read all matching entries

root_cause: ensureQueryData returns cached data or fetches it if missing — handy in loaders/actions

Resolves from cache when fresh, otherwise fetches and caches

root_cause: Invalidating with too-generic keys triggers many unnecessary refetches
fix: Invalidate the narrowest key that covers what changed
Use exact: true when only one entry should refresh
Prefer setQueryData for single-record updates over invalidating whole lists
Batch related invalidations after a mutation in onSettled

root_cause: Control whether a query refetches when a component remounts
fix: refetchOnMount: true (default) refetches if stale on mount
'always' refetches every mount regardless of staleness
false never refetches on mount (serve cache)

root_cause: Real-time feeds should push into the cache rather than poll
fix: Subscribe to the socket in a useEffect
On message: queryClient.setQueryData(key, (old) => applyEvent(old, msg))
Or invalidateQueries(key) for a simpler (but networked) refresh

root_cause: By default refetching an infinite query refetches every loaded page, which can be expensive
fix: Set maxPages to cap how many pages are kept/refetched
Provide getPreviousPageParam if you cap and scroll both directions

Consider setQueryData patches for cheap single-item updates instead

root_cause: Choosing the right flag avoids spinners during background refetches
fix: isPending: true only until the first data arrives (no cached data yet)
isFetching: true whenever a request is in flight, including background refetches

isRefetching = isFetching && !isPending

root_cause: Invalidating per-item in a loop causes redundant refetches; batch by predicate instead
fix: Use a predicate: invalidateQueries({ predicate: (q) => q.queryKey[0] === 'todos' })
One invalidate call marks all matching queries at once
Do bulk mutations server-side and invalidate once afterward
Avoid invalidating inside a tight client loop

root_cause: Both cache server state; they differ in feature depth and API surface
fix: TanStack Query: richer feature set (mutations, infinite queries, devtools, query cancellation, fine cache control)
SWR: lighter, simpler API, great for read-mostly apps

symptoms: how to use Cerebras inference in Next.js
root_cause: Cerebras Inference is an OpenAI-compatible API; you need the SDK and an API key

npm install @cerebras/cerebras_cloud_sdk
const client = new Cerebras({ apiKey: process.env.CEREBRAS_API_KEY })
Call client.chat.completions.create({ model: 'llama-3.3-70b', messages })

root_cause: The Vercel AI SDK has a first-party Cerebras provider for a unified API

import { cerebras } from '@ai-sdk/cerebras'

root_cause: Streaming gives instant token output; Cerebras is extremely fast so streaming feels near-instant

root_cause: Existing OpenAI SDK code can target Cerebras by overriding baseURL and key

root_cause: Picking the right model balances speed, quality, and context length

llama3.1-8b: fastest/cheapest for simple tasks
Larger/newer options (e.g. Llama 4, Qwen 3, gpt-oss) appear over time — check the models endpoint
GET https://api.cerebras.ai/v1/models lists what's currently available to your account
Match model to task: 8b for classification/routing, 70b for reasoning/generation

root_cause: Cerebras runs on wafer-scale hardware delivering very high tokens/sec, which changes UX assumptions
fix: Expect far higher throughput (often 1000s of tokens/sec) than typical GPU inference
This makes streaming feel instant and enables agentic multi-step loops in real time
You can afford more reasoning tokens / longer chains within a request budget
Still stream to the client for the best perceived latency

root_cause: The API key must be configured as a server env var in every Vercel environment

Do NOT prefix with NEXT_PUBLIC_ — it must stay server-only

root_cause: Calling the inference API directly from the client leaks the API key to anyone
fix: Never instantiate the Cerebras client in a Client Component
Proxy through a Next.js route handler or server action that holds the key
Apply auth + rate limiting on your proxy route
Stream the proxied response back to the browser

root_cause: Cerebras enforces per-minute request/token limits; bursts return HTTP 429
fix: Read rate-limit headers to see remaining quota and reset time
Implement exponential backoff + retry on 429
Queue or debounce client-triggered generations
Add your own per-user rate limiting (e.g. @upstash/ratelimit) before hitting the API
Request higher limits from Cerebras if you consistently hit them

root_cause: Cerebras supports OpenAI-style tool calling for agentic workflows

With the AI SDK use generateText({ model: cerebras(...), tools, maxSteps })
Parse tool_calls from the response and execute them server-side
Feed tool results back as tool messages and call again
Pick a model that supports tool calling (e.g. llama-3.3-70b)

root_cause: Force valid JSON either with response_format or the AI SDK's generateObject
fix: AI SDK: const { object } = await generateObject({ model: cerebras('llama-3.3-70b'), schema: zodSchema, prompt })
OpenAI-compat: response_format: { type: 'json_object' } (or json_schema where supported)
Validate the result with Zod even when using JSON mode
Keep schemas small and explicit for reliable extraction

root_cause: Buffering proxies or non-streaming responses break token-by-token delivery on Vercel
fix: Return the SDK's streaming Response (toDataStreamResponse / toAIStreamResponse)
Do not await/collect the full text before responding
Edge runtime streams reliably; Node runtime also streams with the Web Streams response
Ensure no middleware/CDN buffers the response

root_cause: LLM routes can exceed Vercel's default function timeout under load
fix: Cerebras is fast, but set export const maxDuration = 30 (or higher on Pro) as a safety margin
Streaming responses keep the connection alive while tokens flow
Add a client-side abort/timeout for stuck requests
Hobby plan caps duration lower than Pro — size accordingly

symptoms: build a chatbot with Cerebras and useChat
root_cause: A full chat app pairs a streaming route with the useChat hook
fix: Route: export async function POST(req) { const { messages } = await req.json(); return streamText({ model: cerebras('llama-3.3-70b'), messages }).toDataStreamResponse() }
Client: const { messages, input, handleSubmit, handleInputChange } = useChat()
Render messages and a form bound to input/handleSubmit

root_cause: Token usage drives cost; control it with limits and model choice
fix: Set max_tokens / maxOutputTokens to cap output length
Use the smallest model that meets quality (8b for simple tasks)
Trim/summarize conversation history before sending
Log usage from the response (prompt/completion tokens) for monitoring
Add per-user rate limits to prevent abuse

root_cause: A 401 means the API key is missing, wrong, or not loaded into the environment
fix: Verify CEREBRAS_API_KEY is set and matches a key from cloud.cerebras.ai
Confirm the server process actually has the env var (log presence, not the value)
Check for trailing whitespace/quotes in the .env value
Regenerate the key if it may have been revoked or leaked

root_cause: Generation parameters tune determinism and length
fix: temperature (0–1.5ish): lower = more deterministic, higher = more creative
top_p: nucleus sampling alternative to temperature
max_tokens / maxOutputTokens: cap the response length
seed (where supported) for more reproducible outputs
Set them in the create() call or AI SDK options

root_cause: Cerebras handles the generation step; embeddings/retrieval come from another service
fix: Embed documents (Cerebras focuses on chat completions; use an embeddings provider)
Store vectors in pgvector/Pinecone/Upstash Vector

root_cause: High throughput makes multi-step agent loops practical within one request

Still bound total steps/tokens to control cost

root_cause: Using a wrong or unavailable model id returns a not-found error
fix: Fetch GET /v1/models to list ids available to your account
Use the exact id string (e.g. 'llama-3.3-70b', 'llama3.1-8b')

Centralize the model id in one config constant for easy updates

root_cause: Production LLM calls need timeouts, retries, and graceful fallbacks

root_cause: Chat completions use role-tagged messages; the system message steers behavior

symptoms: rate limit and auth LLM endpoint

Aggregate to monitor spend and detect anomalies

root_cause: Streaming AI routes can run on Edge for lower latency if dependencies are edge-compatible

Always keep the system prompt and most recent user message
Trim before sending, not after the error
